text.Base: He who is evil to himself, to whom will he be good? He will not enjoy his own riches.
text.KJV: 14:5 Hee that is euill to himselfe, to whom will he be good? he shall not take pleasure in his goods.
text.DRV: 14:5 He that is wicked to himself, to what other man will he be good? And he shall have no pleasure in his goods.
text.NABRE: 14:5 To whom will they be generous that are stingy with themselves and do not enjoy what is their own?

text.Base: My child, if you have anything, do good to yourself, and bring worthy offerings to the Lord.
text.KJV: 14:11 My sonne, according to thy habilitie doe good to thy selfe, and giue the Lord his due offering.
text.DRV: 14:11 Son if thou have it, do good to thyself, and offer to God worthy oblations.
text.NABRE: 14:11 My son, if you have the means, treat yourself well, and enjoy life as best you can.

text.Base: Remember that death does not tarry, and the decree of Hades has not been shown to you.
text.KJV: 14:12 Remember that death will not be long in comming, and that the couenant of the graue is not shewed vnto thee.
text.DRV: 14:12 Be mindful that death slacked not, and that the covenant of hell hath been shewed thee: for the covenant of this world shall die the death.
text.NABRE: 14:12 Remember that death does not delay, and you have not been told the grave’s appointed time.

text.Base: Do good to friends before you die, and reach out and give to them as much as you can.
text.KJV: 14:13 Doe good vnto thy friend before thou die, and according to thy abilitie, stretch out thy hand and giue to him.
text.DRV: 14:13 Before death do good to thy friend, and according to thine ability stretching out thy hand, give to the poor.
text.NABRE: 14:13 Before you die, be good to your friends; give them a share in what you possess.

text.Base: All living beings grow old like a garment; for the decree from of old is, “You must surely die!”
text.KJV: 14:17 All flesh waxeth old as a garment: for the couenant from the beginning is; thou shalt die the death.
text.DRV: 14:17 Before thy death work justice: for in hell there cannot meat be found.
text.NABRE: 14:17 All flesh grows old like a garment; the age-old law is: everyone must die.

text.Base: Like abundant leaves on a spreading tree that sheds some and puts forth others, so are the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another is born.
text.KJV: 14:18 As of the greene leaues on a thicke tree, some fall, and some grow; so is the generation of flesh and blood, one commeth to an end, and another is borne.
text.DRV: 14:18 All flesh shall wax old as grass, and as the leaf fructifying on a green tree.
text.NABRE: 14:18 As with the leaves growing on a luxuriant tree— one falls off and another sprouts— So with the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another flourishes.

text.Base: Happy is the person who meditates on wisdom and reasons with intelligent understanding,
text.KJV: 14:20 Blessed is the man that doeth meditate good things in wisdome, and that reasoneth of holy things by his vnderstanding.
text.DRV: 14:20 All corruptible work shall fail in the end: and he that worketh it shall go therewith.
text.NABRE: 14:20 The Search for Wisdom and Her Blessings - Happy those who meditate on Wisdom, and fix their gaze on knowledge;

text.Base: who pursues her like a hunter, and lies in wait on her paths;
text.KJV: 14:22 Goe after her as one that traceth, and lie in wait in her wayes.
text.DRV: 14:22 Blessed is the man that shall continue in wisdom, and that shall meditate in his justice, and in understanding shall consider the providence of God.
text.NABRE: 14:22 Who pursue her like a scout, and watch at her entry way;

text.Base: he will place his children under her shelter, and will dwell under her branches;
text.KJV: 14:26 He shal set his children vnder her shelter, and shall lodge vnder her branches.
text.DRV: 14:26 He shall set his children under her covering, and shall abide under her boughs:
text.NABRE: 14:26 Who build their nest in her leaves, and lodge in her branches;

text.Base: he will be sheltered by her from the heat, and will dwell in the midst of her glory.
text.KJV: 14:27 By her he shall be couered from heat, and in her glory shall he dwell.
text.DRV: 14:27 he shall be protected under her covering from the heat, and shall rest in her glory.
text.NABRE: 14:27 Who take refuge from the heat in her shade and dwell in her home.

text.Base: Whoever fears the Lord will do this, and whoever holds to the law will obtain wisdom.
text.KJV: 15:1 He that feareth the Lord will doe good, and he that hath the knowledge of the Law shal obtaine her.
text.DRV: 15:1 He that feareth God, shall do good things, and he that hath justice shall apprehend her,
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - Whoever fears the LORD will do this; whoever is practiced in the Law will come to Wisdom.

text.Base: She will come to meet him like a mother, and like a young bride she will welcome him.
text.KJV: 15:2 And as a mother shall she meet him, and receiue him as a wife maried of a virgin.
text.DRV: 15:2 and she will meet him as an honourable mother, & a wife from virginity she will receive him.
text.NABRE: 15:2 She will meet him like a mother; like a young bride she will receive him,

text.Base: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.
text.KJV: 15:3 With the bread of vnderstanding shall she feed him, and giue him the water of wisedome to drinke.
text.DRV: 15:3 She shall feed him with the bread of life and understanding, and give him the water of wholesome wisdom to drink: and she shall be made sure in him, and he shall not be bowed:
text.NABRE: 15:3 She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of understanding to drink.

text.Base: He will find gladness and a crown of rejoicing, and will acquire an everlasting name.
text.KJV: 15:6 He shall finde ioy, and a crowne of gladnesse, and she shall cause him to inherit an euerlasting name.
text.DRV: 15:6 Joy & exultation she shall heap upon him, & shall make him inherit an everlasting name.
text.NABRE: 15:6 Joy and gladness he will find, and an everlasting name he will inherit.

text.Base: Do not say, “It was the Lord’s doing that I fell away”; for he does not do what he hates.
text.KJV: 15:11 Say not thou, It is through the Lord, that I fell away, for thou oughtest not to doe the things that he hateth.
text.DRV: 15:11 Say not: It is by God, that she is absent: for do not thou the things that he hateth.
text.NABRE: 15:11 Free Will - Do not say: “It was God’s doing that I fell away,” for what he hates he does not do.

text.Base: Do not say, “It was he who led me astray”; for he has no need of the sinful.
text.KJV: 15:12 Say not thou, He hath caused mee to erre, for hee hath no need of the sinfull man.
text.DRV: 15:12 Say not: He hath made me err: for impious men are not necessary for him.
text.NABRE: 15:12 Do not say: “He himself has led me astray,” for he has no need of the wicked.

text.Base: The Lord hates all abominations; such things are not loved by those who fear him.
text.KJV: 15:13 The Lord hateth all abomination, and they that feare God loue it not.
text.DRV: 15:13 Our Lord hateth all abomination of error, and it shall not be amiable to them, that fear him.
text.NABRE: 15:13 Abominable wickedness the LORD hates and he does not let it happen to those who fear him.

text.Base: It was he who created humankind in the beginning, and he left them in the power of their own free choice.
text.KJV: 15:14 Hee himselfe made man from the beginning, and left him in the hand of his counsell,
text.DRV: 15:14 God from the beginning made man, and left him in the hand of his own counsel.
text.NABRE: 15:14 God in the beginning created human beings and made them subject to their own free choice.

text.Base: If you choose, you can keep the commandments, and to act faithfully is a matter of your own choice.
text.KJV: 15:15 If thou wilt, to keepe the Commandements, and to performe acceptable faithfulnesse.
text.DRV: 15:15 He added his commandments and precepts.
text.NABRE: 15:15 If you choose, you can keep the commandments; loyalty is doing the will of God.

text.Base: He has placed before you fire and water; stretch out your hand for whichever you choose.
text.KJV: 15:16 He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thy hand vnto whether thou wilt.
text.DRV: 15:16 If thou will keep the commandment, and keep acceptable fidelity for ever, they shall preserve thee.
text.NABRE: 15:16 Set before you are fire and water; to whatever you choose, stretch out your hand.

text.Base: Before each person are life and death, and whichever one chooses will be given.
text.KJV: 15:17 Before man is life and death, and whether him liketh shalbe giuen him.
text.DRV: 15:17 He hath set before thee water and fire: to which thou wilt, stretch forth thine hand.
text.NABRE: 15:17 Before everyone are life and death, whichever they choose will be given them.

text.Base: For great is the wisdom of the Lord; he is mighty in power and sees everything;
text.KJV: 15:18 For the wisedome of the Lord is great, and he is mighty in power, and beholdeth all things,
text.DRV: 15:18 Before man there is life and death, good and evil: what pleaseth him shall be given him:
text.NABRE: 15:18 Immense is the wisdom of the LORD; mighty in power, he sees all things.

text.Base: his eyes are on those who fear him, and he knows every human action.
text.KJV: 15:19 And his eyes are vpon them that feare him, & hee knoweth euery worke of man.
text.DRV: 15:19 Because the wisdom of God is much, and he is strong in might, seeing all men without intermission.
text.NABRE: 15:19 The eyes of God behold his works, and he understands every human deed.

text.Base: He has not commanded anyone to be wicked, and he has not given anyone permission to sin.
text.KJV: 15:20 Hee hath commanded no man to do wickedly, neither hath he giuen any man license to sinne.
text.DRV: 15:20 The eyes of our Lord are towards them that fear him, and he knoweth all the work of man.
text.NABRE: 15:20 He never commands anyone to sin, nor shows leniency toward deceivers.

text.Base: Do not desire a multitude of worthless children, and do not rejoice in ungodly offspring.
text.KJV: 16:1 Desire not a multitude of vnprofitable children, neither delight in vngodly sonnes.
text.DRV: 16:1 Rejoice not in impious children, if they be multiplied: neither be delighted upon them, if the fear of God be not in them.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 God’s Punishment of Sinners - Do not yearn for worthless children, or rejoice in wicked offspring.

text.Base: If they multiply, do not rejoice in them, unless the fear of the Lord is in them.
text.KJV: 16:2 Though they multiply, reioyce not in them, except the feare of the Lord be with them.
text.DRV: 16:2 Credit not their life, and respect not their labours.
text.NABRE: 16:2 Even if they be many, do not rejoice in them if they do not have fear of the LORD.

text.Base: In a gathering of sinners a fire is kindled, and in a disobedient nation wrath blazes up.
text.KJV: 16:6 In the congregation of the vngodly, shall a fire be kindled, and in a rebellious nation, wrath is set on fire.
text.DRV: 16:6 Many such things hath mine eye seen, and mine ear hath heard things of more force than these.
text.NABRE: 16:6 Against a sinful band fire is kindled, upon a godless people wrath blazes.

text.Base: He did not forgive the ancient giants who revolted in their might.
text.KJV: 16:7 Hee was not pacified towards the olde giants, who fell away in the strength of their foolishnesse.
text.DRV: 16:7 In the synagogue of sinners a fire shall flame, and in an incredulous nation wrath shall wax hot.
text.NABRE: 16:7 He did not forgive the princes of old who rebelled long ago in their might.

text.Base: And if there is even one stubborn person, it is a wonder if he could go unpunished. For mercy and wrath are with the Lord; he is mighty to forgive, and he pours out wrath.
text.KJV: 16:11 And if there be one stiffe-necked among the people, it is marueile, if he escape vnpunished; for mercy and wrath are with him, hee is mighty to forgiue, and to powre out displeasure.
text.DRV: 16:11 And as the six hundred thousand foot men, which were gathered together in the hardness of their heart: and if one had been stiff necked, it is marvel if he had been unpunished.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Had there been but one stiff-necked person, it would be a wonder had he gone unpunished. For mercy and anger alike are with him; he remits and forgives, but also pours out wrath.

text.Base: As great as is his mercy, so also is his chastisement; he judges a person according to one’s deeds.
text.KJV: 16:12 As his mercy is great, so is his correction also: he iudgeth a man according to his workes.
text.DRV: 16:12 For there is mercy and wrath with him. Mighty exoration, & pouring out wrath:
text.NABRE: 16:12 Great as his mercy is his punishment; he judges people, each according to their deeds.

text.Base: Do not say, “I am hidden from the Lord, and who from on high has me in mind? Among so many people I am unknown, for what am I in a boundless creation?”
text.KJV: 16:17 Say not thou, I will hide my selfe from the Lord: shall any remember me from aboue? I shall not be remembred among so many people: for what is my soule among such an infinite number of creatures?
text.DRV: 16:17 In a great people I shall not be known: for what is my soul among so innumerable creatures?

text.Base: Behold, heaven and the highest heaven, the abyss and the earth, tremble at his visitation!
text.KJV: 16:18 Behold, the heauen, and the heauen of heauens, the deepe and the earth, and all that therein is, shall be mooued when he shall visit.
text.DRV: 16:18 Behold heaven, and the heavens of heavens, the depth, and all the earth, & the things that are in them, in his sight shall be moved,

text.Base: When the Lord created his works from the beginning, and, in making them, determined their boundaries,
text.KJV: 16:26 The works of the Lord are done in iudgement from the beginning: and from the time he made them, hee disposed the parts thereof.
text.DRV: 16:26 In the judgement of God are his works from the beginning, and from the institution of them he distinguished their parts, and their beginnings in their nations.

text.Base: he arranged his works in an eternal order, and their dominion for all generations of them. They neither hunger nor grow weary, and they do not abandon their tasks.
text.KJV: 16:27 Hee garnished his workes for euer, and in his hand are the chiefe of them vnto all generations: they neither labour, nor are weary, nor cease from their workes.
text.DRV: 16:27 He adorned their works for ever, neither have they hungered, nor laboured, and they have not ceased from their works.

text.Base: The Lord created human beings out of earth, and makes them return to it again.
text.KJV: 17:1 The Lord created man of the earth, and turned him into it againe.
text.DRV: 17:1 God created man of the earth, and after his own image he made him.
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 Creation of Human Beings - The Lord created human beings from the earth, and makes them return to earth again.

text.Base: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his own image.
text.KJV: 17:3 He endued them with strength by themselues, and made them according to his image,
text.DRV: 17:3 He gave him a number of days and time, and gave him power of those things that are upon the earth.
text.NABRE: 17:3 He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his image.

text.Base: He filled them with knowledge and understanding, and showed them good and evil.
text.KJV: 17:7 Withall, hee filled them with the knowledge of vnderstanding, & shewed them good and euill.
text.DRV: 17:7 He set his eye upon their hearts to shew them the great things of his works:

text.Base: He bestowed upon them knowledge, and gave them the law of life as a heritage.
text.KJV: 17:11 Beside this he gaue them knowledge, and the law of life for an heritage.
text.DRV: 17:11 And their eye saw the glorious things of his honour, & their ears heard the honour of his voice, and he said to them: Beware of every unjust thing.

text.Base: He established with them an eternal covenant, and revealed to them his decrees.
text.KJV: 17:12 He made an euerlasting couenant with them, and shewed them his iudgements.
text.DRV: 17:12 And he gave them commandment every one concerning his neighbour.

text.Base: Their ways are always before him; they will not be hidden from his eyes.
text.KJV: 17:15 Their wayes are euer before him, and shall not be hid from his eyes.
text.DRV: 17:15 And Israel was made the manifest portion of God.

text.Base: All their works are as the sun before him, and his eyes are continually upon their ways.
text.KJV: 17:19 Therefore all their workes are as the Sunne before him, and his eyes are continually vpon their wayes.
text.DRV: 17:19 And afterward he shall arise, and shall render them reward, to every one upon their head, and shall turn into the inner parts of the earth.

text.Base: But to those who repent he grants a return, and he encourages those who are losing their hope.
text.KJV: 17:24 But vnto them that repent, he granted them returne, and comforted those that faile in patience.
text.DRV: 17:24 and know the justices and judgments of God, and stand in the lot of thy purpose, and of prayer of the most high God.

text.Base: Turn to the Lord and forsake your sins; pray in his presence and lessen your offense.
text.KJV: 17:25 Returne vnto the Lord, and forsake thy sinnes, make thy prayer before his face, and offend lesse.
text.DRV: 17:25 Go into the parts of the holy world, with the living, and them that give praise to God.

text.Base: Return to the Most High and turn away from iniquity, and hate intensely what he abhors.
text.KJV: 17:26 Turne againe to the most High, and turne away from iniquitie: for he will leade thee out of darkenesse into the light of health, and hate thou abomination vehemently.
text.DRV: 17:26 Tarry not in the error of the impious, before death confess. From the dead as nothing, confession perisheth.

text.Base: How great is the mercy of the Lord, and his forgiveness for those who return to him!
text.KJV: 17:28 Thankesgiuing perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not: the liuing and sound in heart, shall praise the Lord.
text.DRV: 17:28 How great is the mercy of our Lord, and his propitiation to them that turn to him!

text.Base: He who lives forever created the whole universe.
text.KJV: 18:1 Hee that liueth for euer, created all things in generall.
text.DRV: 18:1 He that liveth for ever, created all things together. God only shall be justified, and remaineth an invincible King for ever.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 The Divine Power and Mercy - He who lives forever created the whole universe;

text.Base: What are mortals, and of what use are they? What is their good and what is their evil?
text.KJV: 18:8 What is man, and whereto serueth he? what is his good, & what is his euil?
text.DRV: 18:8 The number of the days of men at the most an hundred years: as drops of the water of the sea they are reputed: and as the gravel stone of the sand, so a few years in the day of eternity.

text.Base: The number of their days is great if they reach a hundred years.
text.KJV: 18:9 The number of a mans dayes at the most are an hundred yeeres.
text.DRV: 18:9 For this cause God is patient toward them, and poureth out his mercy upon them.

text.Base: Like a drop of water from the sea and a grain of sand, so are a few years among the days of eternity.
text.KJV: 18:10 As a drop of water vnto the Sea, and a grauell stone in comparison of the sand, so are a thousand yeeres to the dayes of eternitie.
text.DRV: 18:10 He hath seen the presumption of their heart that it is naught, and hath known their subversion that it is evil.

text.Base: Therefore the Lord is patient with them and pours out his mercy upon them.
text.KJV: 18:11 Therfore is God patient with them, & powreth forth his mercy vpon them.
text.DRV: 18:11 Therefore hath he fulfilled his propitiation toward them, and hath shewed them the way of equity.

text.Base: The compassion of human beings is for their neighbors, but the compassion of the Lord is for every living thing.
text.KJV: 18:13 The mercy of man is toward his neighbour, but the mercy of the Lord is vpon all flesh: he reprooueth and nurtureth, and teacheth, & bringeth againe as a shepheard his flocke.
text.DRV: 18:13 He that hath mercy, teacheth and instructeth, as a pastor his flock.

text.Base: My child, do not mix reproach with your good deeds, or bitter words with any gift.
text.KJV: 18:15 My sonne, blemish not thy good deeds, neither vse vncomfortable words when thou giuest any thing.
text.DRV: 18:15 Son in good deeds give no blame, & in every gift give not the sadness of an evil word.

text.Base: Before you speak, learn; and before you fall sick, take care of yourself.
text.KJV: 18:19 Learne before thou speake, and vse phisicke, or euer thou be sicke.
text.DRV: 18:19 Before judgment prepare thee justice, and before thou speak learn.

text.Base: Before judgment comes, examine yourself; and at the time of scrutiny you will find forgiveness.
text.KJV: 18:20 Before iudgement examine thy selfe, and in the day of visitation thou shalt find mercy.
text.DRV: 18:20 Before sickness take medicine, and before judgement examine thyself, and in the sight of God thou shalt find propitiation.

text.Base: Do not follow your base desires, but restrain your appetites.
text.KJV: 18:30 Goe not after thy lustes, but refraine thy selfe from thine appetites.
text.DRV: 18:30 Go not after thy concupiscences, and turn away from thy will.

text.Base: A worker who is a drunkard will never become rich; one who despises small things will fail little by little.
text.KJV: 19:1 A labouring man that is giuen to drunkennesse shal not be rich, and hee that contemneth small things shall fall by little & little.
text.DRV: 19:1 Aworkman that is a drunkard shall not be rich: and he that contemneth small things, shall fall by little and little.
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Whoever does this grows no richer; those who waste the little they have will be stripped bare.

text.Base: Wine and women lead intelligent men astray, and the man who consorts with prostitutes is reckless.
text.KJV: 19:2 Wine and women will make men of vnderstanding to fall away, and he that cleaueth to harlots will become impudent.
text.DRV: 19:2 Wine and women make wisemen to apostatate, and shall reprove the prudent:
text.NABRE: 19:2 Wine and women make the heart lustful, and the companion of prostitutes becomes reckless.

text.Base: One who trusts others too quickly is light-minded, and one who sins does wrong to himself.
text.KJV: 19:4 He that is hasty to giue credit is light minded, and he that sinneth shall offend against his owne soule.
text.DRV: 19:4 He that giveth credit quickly, is light of heart, and shall be lessened: and he shall moreover be counted one that sinneth against his own soul.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Whoever trusts others too quickly has a shallow mind, and those who sin wrong themselves.

text.Base: Question a friend; perhaps he did not do it; or if he did, so that he may not do it again.
text.KJV: 19:13 Admonish a friend, it may be he hath not done it, and if he haue that he doe it no more.
text.DRV: 19:13 Rebuke a friend, lest perhaps he hath not understood, and say: I did it not: or if he did it, that he do it not again.
text.NABRE: 19:13 Admonish your friend—he may not have done it; and if he did, that he may not do it again.

text.Base: Question a neighbor; perhaps he did not say it; or if he said it, so that he may not repeat it.
text.KJV: 19:14 Admonish thy friend, it may be he hath not said it, and if he haue, that he speake it not againe.
text.DRV: 19:14 Rebuke thy neighbour, lest perhaps he said it not: and if he said it, lest perhaps he iterate it.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Admonish your neighbor—he may not have said it; and if he did, that he may not say it again.

text.Base: Question a friend, for often it is only slander; do not believe everything you hear.
text.KJV: 19:15 Admonish a friend: for many times it is a slander, & beleeue not euery tale.
text.DRV: 19:15 Rebuke thy friend: for there is often a fault committed.
text.NABRE: 19:15 Admonish your friend—often it may be slander; do not believe every story.

text.Base: All wisdom is the fear of the Lord, and in all wisdom there is the fulfillment of the law.
text.KJV: 19:20 The feare of the Lord is all wisedome, and in all wisedome is the performance of the Law, and the knowledge of his omnipotencie.
text.DRV: 19:20 There is wickedness, and in it execration: and there is a fool that hath less wisdom.

text.Base: Better are the God-fearing who lack understanding than the highly intelligent who transgress the law.
text.KJV: 19:24 He that hath smal vnderstanding and feareth God, is better then one that hath much wisedome, and transgresseth the Law of the most High.
text.DRV: 19:24 and there is a just man that submitteth himself overmuch of great humility: and there is a just one that boweth his face, and feigneth himself not to see that which is unknown:

text.Base: There is a rebuke that is untimely, and there is the person who is wise enough to keep silent.
text.KJV: 20:1 There is a reproofe that is not comely: againe some man holdeth his tongue, and he is wise.
text.DRV: 20:1 How good a thing is it to rebuke, rather than to be angry, and not to hinder him that confesseth in prayer!
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 Conduct of the Wise and the Foolish - There is an admonition that is untimely, but the silent person is the wise one.

text.Base: How much better it is to rebuke than to fume!
text.KJV: 20:2 It is much better to reprooue, then to be angry secretly, and he that confesseth his fault, shall be preserued from hurt.
text.DRV: 20:2 The concupiscence of an eunuch shall deflower a young-maid:
text.NABRE: 20:2 It is much better to admonish than to lose one’s temper;

text.Base: One person keeps silent and is found to be wise; another is detested for being talkative.
text.KJV: 20:5 There is one that keepeth silence and is found wise: and another by much babling becommeth hatefull.
text.DRV: 20:5 There is that holdeth his peace, which is found wise: and there is that is odious, he which is malapert to speak.
text.NABRE: 20:5 One is silent and is thought wise; another, for being talkative, is disliked.

text.Base: A wise person will keep silent until the right moment, but a boasting fool misses the right moment.
text.KJV: 20:7 A wise man wil hold his tongue till he see opportunitie: but a babler and a foole will regard no time.
text.DRV: 20:7 Awise man will hold his peace until a time: but a wanton, and the unwise will not observe time.
text.NABRE: 20:7 The wise remain silent till the right time comes, but a boasting fool misses the proper time.

text.Base: A slip on the pavement is better than a slip of the tongue; so the downfall of the wicked will come speedily.
text.KJV: 20:18 To slip vpon a pauement, is better then to slip with the tongue: so, the fall of the wicked shall come speedily.
text.DRV: 20:18 For they that eat his bread, are of a false tongue. How often, and how many will laugh him to scorn?
text.NABRE: 20:18 A slip on the floor is better than a slip of the tongue; in like manner the downfall of the wicked comes quickly.

text.Base: A lie is a foul blot on a person; it is continually on the mouth of the ignorant.
text.KJV: 20:24 A lie is a foule blot in a man, yet it is continually in the mouth of the vntaught.
text.DRV: 20:24 There is that will destroy his soul for shamefastness, and by an unwise person he will destroy it: and by acception of person he will destroy himself.
text.NABRE: 20:24 A lie is a foul blot in a person, yet it is always on the lips of the ignorant.

text.Base: The wise person advances himself by his words, and one who is sensible pleases the great.
text.KJV: 20:27 A wise man shall promote himselfe to honour with his words: and hee that hath vnderstanding, will please great men.
text.DRV: 20:27 Better is a thief, than the continual custom of a lying man, but both shall inherit perdition.
text.NABRE: 20:27 The wise gain promotion with few words, the prudent please the great.

text.Base: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure, of what value is either?
text.KJV: 20:30 Wisedome that is hidde, and treasure that is hoarded vp, what profit is in them both?
text.DRV: 20:30 He that tilleth his land, shall make an high heap of corn: and who so worketh justice, he shall be exalted: and he that pleaseth great men, shall avoid iniquity.
text.NABRE: 20:30 Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure— what value has either?

text.Base: Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom.
text.KJV: 20:31 Better is he that hideth his folly, then a man that hideth his wisedome.
text.DRV: 20:31 Presents & gifts blind the eyes of judges, and as one dumb in the mouth turneth away their chastisements.
text.NABRE: 20:31 Better are those who hide their folly than those who hide their wisdom.

text.Base: Have you sinned, my child? Do so no more, but ask forgiveness for your past sins.
text.KJV: 21:1 My sonne, hast thou sinned? doe so no more, but aske pardon for thy former sinnes.
text.DRV: 21:1 Son hast thou sinned? Do so no more: but for the old also pray that they may be forgiven thee.
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 Dangers from Sin - My child, if you have sinned, do so no more, and for your past sins pray to be forgiven.

text.Base: Flee from sin as from a snake; for if you approach it, it will bite you; its teeth are lion’s teeth, and can destroy human lives.
text.KJV: 21:2 Flee from sinne as from the face of a Serpent: for if thou commest too neere it, it will bite thee: the teeth thereof, are as the teeth of a lyon, slaying the soules of men.
text.DRV: 21:2 As from the face of a serpent fly from sins: and if thou approach to them they will receive thee.
text.NABRE: 21:2 Flee from sin as from a serpent that will bite you if you go near it; Its teeth, lion’s teeth, destroying human lives.

text.Base: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; there is no healing for the wound it inflicts.
text.KJV: 21:3 All iniquitie is as a two edged sword, the wounds whereof cannot be healed.
text.DRV: 21:3 The teeth of a lion the teeth thereof, killing the souls of men.
text.NABRE: 21:3 All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; when it cuts, there is no healing.

text.Base: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgment comes speedily.
text.KJV: 21:5 A prayer out of a poore mans mouth reacheth to the eares of God, and his iudgement commeth speedily.
text.DRV: 21:5 Brawling and injuries shall bring the substance to nothing: and the house that is very rich, shall be made nothing by pride: so the substance of the proud shall be rooted out.
text.NABRE: 21:5 Prayer from the lips of the poor is heard at once, and justice is quickly granted them.

text.Base: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.
text.KJV: 21:10 The way of sinners is made plaine with stones, but at the ende thereof is the pit of hell.
text.DRV: 21:10 The synagogue of sinners is as tow gathered together, and their consummation a flame of fire.
text.NABRE: 21:10 The path of sinners is smooth stones, but its end is the pit of Sheol.

text.Base: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it;
text.KJV: 21:15 If a skilfull man heare a wise word, hee will commend it, and adde vnto it: but assoone as one of no vnderstanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behinde his backe.
text.DRV: 21:15 But there is wisdom that aboundeth in evil: and there is no understanding where bitterness is.
text.NABRE: 21:15 When the intelligent hear a wise saying, they praise it and add to it. The wanton hear it with distaste and cast it behind their back.

text.Base: The chatter of a fool is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.
text.KJV: 21:16 The talking of a foole is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.
text.DRV: 21:16 The knowledge of the wise shall abound as an inundation, and his counsel is permanent as a fountain of life.
text.NABRE: 21:16 A fool’s chatter is like a load on a journey, but delight is to be found on the lips of the intelligent.

text.Base: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but a clever person smiles quietly.
text.KJV: 21:20 A foole lifteth vp his voyce with laughter, but a wise man doeth scarce smile a litle.
text.DRV: 21:20 The mouth of the prudent is sought in the Church, and they will think upon his words in their hearts.
text.NABRE: 21:20 Fools raise their voice in laughter, but the prudent at most smile quietly.

text.Base: The heart of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.KJV: 21:26 The heart of fooles is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.DRV: 21:26 Afool will look from the window into the house: but he nurtured will stand without.
text.NABRE: 21:26 The mind of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.

text.Base: The sluggard is like a filthy stone, and everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.KJV: 22:1 A slouthful man is compared to a filthy stone, and euery one will hisse him out to his disgrace.
text.DRV: 22:1 The sluggard is stoned with a dirty stone, & all men will speak of his disgrace.
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 On Laziness and Foolishness - The sluggard is like a filthy stone; everyone hisses at his disgrace.

text.Base: It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.
text.KJV: 22:3 An euill nurtured sonne is the dishonour of his father that begate him: and a daughter is borne to his losse.
text.DRV: 22:3 The confusion of the father is of a son without discipline: and the daughter shall be made of less account.
text.NABRE: 22:3 An undisciplined child is a disgrace to its father; if it be a daughter, she brings him to poverty.

text.Base: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.
text.KJV: 22:4 A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but shee that liueth dishonestly, is her fathers heauinesse.
text.DRV: 22:4 Awise daughter is an inheritance to her husband: for she that confoundeth, is made a contumely to her father.
text.NABRE: 22:4 A thoughtful daughter obtains a husband of her own; a shameless one is her father’s grief.

text.Base: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.
text.KJV: 22:7 Who so teacheth a foole, is as one that gleweth a potsheard together, and as hee that waketh one from a sound sleepe.
text.DRV: 22:7 He that teacheth a fool, is as he that glueth together a pot-sherd.

text.Base: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind.
text.KJV: 22:11 Weepe for the dead, for hee hath lost the light: and weepe for the foole, for he wanteth vnderstanding: make litle weeping for the dead, for hee is at rest: but the life of the foole is worse then death.
text.DRV: 22:11 Weep a little upon the dead, because he is at rest.

text.Base: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for a fool or an ungodly person it lasts all the days of their lives.
text.KJV: 22:12 Seuen dayes doe men mourne for him that is dead; but for a foole, and an vngodly man, all the dayes of his life.
text.DRV: 22:12 For the wicked life of the very wicked, above the death of a fool.

text.Base: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow—in these cases any friend will flee.
text.KJV: 22:22 If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, feare not, for there may be a reconciliation: except for vpbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound, for, for these things euery friend will depart.
text.DRV: 22:22 so also a fearful heart in the cogitation of a fool shall not resist against the violence of fear.

text.Base: Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?
text.KJV: 22:27 Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seale of wisedome vpon my lippes, that I fall not suddenly by them, & that my tongue destroy me not?
text.DRV: 22:27 If thou open a sad mouth, fear not: for there is agreement: except taunt, and reproach, and pride, and revealing of secret, and a treacherous wound: in all these things a friend will fly away.

text.Base: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!
text.KJV: 23:1 O Lord, father and gouernour of all my whole life, leaue me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.
text.DRV: 23:1 O Lord father, and dominatour of my life, leave me not in their counsel: nor suffer me to fall in them.
text.NABRE: 23:1 Chapter 23 - Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, do not let me fall because of them!

text.Base: Listen, my children, to instruction about the mouth; the one who keeps it will not be caught.
text.KJV: 23:7 Heare, O yee children, the discipline of the mouth: He that keepeth it, shall neuer be taken in his lippes.
text.DRV: 23:7 O children hear the doctrine of the mouth: and he that will keep it, shall not perish by his lips, nor be scandalized in most wicked works.
text.NABRE: 23:7 Proper Use of the Tongue - Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth, for whoever keeps it will not be ensnared.

text.Base: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One.
text.KJV: 23:9 Accustome not thy mouth to swearing: neither vse thy selfe to the naming of the holy one.
text.DRV: 23:9 Let not thy mouth be accustomed to swearing: for there be many falls in it.
text.NABRE: 23:9 Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, or habitually utter the Holy Name.

text.Base: A man who violates his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me; why should I fear? The Most High will not remember my sins.”
text.KJV: 23:18 A man that breaketh wedlocke, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darknesse: the walles couer me; & no body seeth me, what neede I to feare? The most high wil not remember my sinnes:
text.DRV: 23:18 Remember thy father and thy mother, for thou sittest in the midst of great men:
text.NABRE: 23:18 The man who dishonors his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, walls hide me, no one sees me. Who can stop me from sinning?” He is not mindful of the Most High,

text.Base: His fear is confined to human eyes, and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun.
text.KJV: 23:19 Such a man only feareth the eies of men, and knoweth not that the eies of the Lord are tenne thousand times brighter then the Sunne, beholding all the waies of men, and considering the most secret parts.
text.DRV: 23:19 lest perhaps God forget thee in their sight, & being besotted with thy daily custom, thou suffer reproach, & hadst better not have been born, and curse the day of thy nativity.
text.NABRE: 23:19 fearing only human eyes. He does not realize that the eyes of the Lord, ten thousand times brighter than the sun, Observe every step taken and peer into hidden corners.

text.Base: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.
text.KJV: 23:22 Thus shall it goe also with the wife, that leaueth her husband, and bringeth in an heire by another:
text.DRV: 23:22 An hot soul as a burning fire will not be quenched, till it swallow somewhat.
text.NABRE: 23:22 So it is with the woman unfaithful to her husband, who offers him an heir by another man.

text.Base: Those who survive will know that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the Lord’s commandments.
text.KJV: 23:27 And they that remaine, shall know that there is nothing better then the feare of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter then to take heed vnto the Commandement of the Lord.
text.DRV: 23:27 And he understandeth not that his eye seeth all things, for that such fear of man expelleth from him the fear of God, & the eyes of men fearing him:
text.NABRE: 23:27 Thus all who dwell on the earth shall know, all who remain in the world shall understand, That nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, nothing sweeter than obeying the commandments of the Lord.

text.Base: Wisdom praises herself, and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.
text.KJV: 24:1 Wisedome shall praise her selfe, and shall glory in the midst of her people.
text.DRV: 24:1 Wisdom shall praise her soul, & shall be honoured in God, and shall glory in the midst of her people,
text.NABRE: 24:1 Chapter 24 Praise of Wisdom - Wisdom sings her own praises, among her own people she proclaims her glory.

text.Base: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:
text.KJV: 24:2 In the Congregation of the most high, shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.
text.DRV: 24:2 and shall open her mouth in the churches of the Highest, and shall glory in the sight of his power,
text.NABRE: 24:2 In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, in the presence of his host she tells of her glory:

text.Base: “I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.”
text.KJV: 24:3 I came out of the mouth of the most High, and couered the earth as a cloud.
text.DRV: 24:3 and in the midst of her people she shall be exalted, and in the holy assembly she shall be admired,
text.NABRE: 24:3 “From the mouth of the Most High I came forth, and covered the earth like a mist.

text.Base: “Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, ‘Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.’”
text.KJV: 24:8 So the creatour of all things gaue mee a commandement, and hee that made me, caused my tabernacle to rest: and said, Let thy dwelling be in Iacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.
text.DRV: 24:8 I alone have gone round about the compass of heaven, and have penetrated into the bottom of the depth, and have walked in the waves of the sea,
text.NABRE: 24:8 “Then the Creator of all gave me his command, and my Creator chose the spot for my tent. He said, ‘In Jacob make your dwelling, in Israel your inheritance.’

text.Base: “Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.”
text.KJV: 24:9 Hee created mee from the beginning before the world, and I shall neuer faile.
text.DRV: 24:9 and stood in all the earth: and in all people.
text.NABRE: 24:9 Before all ages, from the beginning, he created me, and through all ages I shall not cease to be.

text.Base: “Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.”
text.KJV: 24:19 Come vnto me all ye that be desirous of mee, and fill your selues with my fruits.
text.DRV: 24:19 As a fair olive tree in the fields, and as a plane tree by the water in the streets am I exalted.

text.Base: “For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.”
text.KJV: 24:20 For my memorial is sweeter then hony, and mine inheritance then the hony combe.
text.DRV: 24:20 I gave an odour as cinnamon, & aromatical balm: as chosen myrrh have I given the sweetness of odour:

text.Base: “Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.”
text.KJV: 24:21 They that eate mee shall yet be hungry, and they that drinke me shall yet be thirstie.
text.DRV: 24:21 and as storax, and galbanum, and onyx, and aloes, and as Libanus not cut, have I perfumed mine habitation, and mine odour is as balm not mingled.

text.Base: All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.
text.KJV: 24:23 All these things are the booke of the Couenant of the most high God, euen the Law which Moses commanded for an heritage vnto the Congregations of Iacob.
text.DRV: 24:23 I as a vine have fructified sweetness of odour: and my flowers are fruit of honour and honesty.

text.Base: I take pleasure in three things, and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband who live in harmony.
text.KJV: 25:1 In three things I was beautified, and stoode vp beautiful, both before God and men: the vnitie of brethren, the loue of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.
text.DRV: 25:1 In three things my spirit is pleased, which are approved before God, and men:
text.NABRE: 25:1 Chapter 25 Those Who Are Worthy of Praise - With three things I am delighted, for they are pleasing to the Lord and to human beings: Harmony among relatives, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband living happily together.

text.Base: I detest three kinds of people... a beggar who is proud, a rich man who is a liar, and an adulterous old man who lacks good sense.
text.KJV: 25:2 Three sorts of men my soule hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poore man that is proud, a rich man that is a lyar, and an olde adulterer that doteth.
text.DRV: 25:2 The concord of brethren, and the love of neighbours, and man and wife well agreeing together.
text.NABRE: 25:2 Three kinds of people I hate, and I loathe their manner of life: A proud pauper, a rich liar, and a lecherous old fool.

text.Base: I can think of nine whom I would call blessed, and a tenth my tongue proclaims: a man who can rejoice in his children; a man who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.KJV: 25:7 There be nine things which I haue iudged in mine heart to be happy, and the tenth I will vtter with my tongue: a man that hath ioy of his children, and he that liueth to see the fall of his enemie.
text.DRV: 25:7 How beautiful is wisdom for the aged, & understanding glorious, and counsel!
text.NABRE: 25:7 There are nine who come to mind as blessed, a tenth whom my tongue proclaims: The man who finds joy in his children, and the one who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.

text.Base: Happy is the one who lives with a sensible wife, and the one who does not plow with ox and ass together. Happy is the one who does not slip with the tongue, and the one who has not served a man inferior to himself.
text.KJV: 25:8 Well is him that dwelleth with a wife of vnderstanding, and that hath not slipped with his tongue, and that hath not serued a man more vnworthy then himselfe.
text.DRV: 25:8 Much cunning is the crown of old men, and the fear of God is their glory.
text.NABRE: 25:8 Happy the man who lives with a sensible woman, and the one who does not plow with an ox and a donkey combined. Happy the one who does not sin with the tongue, who does not serve an inferior.

text.Base: Happy is the one who finds a friend, and the one who speaks to attentive listeners.
text.KJV: 25:9 Well is him that hath found prudence, and he that speaketh in the eares of him that will heare.
text.DRV: 25:9 Nine things not to be imagined of the heart have I magnified, and the tenth I will tell unto men with my tongue:
text.NABRE: 25:9 Happy the one who finds a friend, who speaks to attentive ears.

text.Base: How great is the one who finds wisdom! But none is superior to the one who fears the Lord.
text.KJV: 25:10 Oh how great is he that findeth wisedome! yet is there none aboue him that feareth the Lord.
text.DRV: 25:10 Aman that hath joy in his children; living and seeing the subversion of his enemies.
text.NABRE: 25:10 How great is the one who finds wisdom, but none is greater than the one who fears the Lord.

text.Base: From a woman sin had its beginning, and because of her we all die.
text.KJV: 25:24 Of the woman came the beginning of sinne, & through her wee all die.
text.DRV: 25:24 The wickedness of a woman changeth her face: and darkeneth her countenance as a bear: and will shew it as a sack. In the midst of her neighbours,

text.Base: Happy is the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.KJV: 26:1 Blessed is the man that hath a vertuous wife, for the number of his dayes shall be double.
text.DRV: 26:1 The husband of a good wife is happy: for the number of his years is double.
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 - Happy the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.

text.Base: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.KJV: 26:2 A vertuous woman reioyceth her husband, and he shall fulfill the yeeres of his life in peace.
text.DRV: 26:2 Astrong woman delighteth her husband, and shall accomplish the years of his life in peace.
text.NABRE: 26:2 A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.

text.Base: A good wife is a great blessing; she will be granted among the blessings of the man who fears the Lord.
text.KJV: 26:3 A good wife is a good portion, which shall be giuen in the portion of them that feare the Lord.
text.DRV: 26:3 Agood woman is a good portion, in the good portion of them that fear God shall she be given to a man for good deeds:
text.NABRE: 26:3 A good wife is a generous gift bestowed upon him who fears the Lord.

text.Base: A wife’s charm delights her husband, and her skill puts fat on his bones.
text.KJV: 26:13 The grace of a wife delighteth her husband, and her discretion will fat his bones.
text.DRV: 26:13 On thy daughter that turneth not away herself, set sure watch: lest occasion found she abuse herself.
text.NABRE: 26:13 A gracious wife delights her husband; her thoughtfulness puts flesh on his bones.

text.Base: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord, and nothing is so precious as her self-discipline.
text.KJV: 26:14 A silent and louing woman is a gift of the Lord, and there is nothing so much worth, as a mind well instructed.
text.DRV: 26:14 Take heed of the impudency of her eyes, and marvel not if she contemn thee.
text.NABRE: 26:14 A silent wife is a gift from the Lord; nothing is worth more than her self-discipline.

text.Base: Two things grieve my heart, and a third makes me angry: a warrior in want through poverty, intelligent men who are treated with contempt, and a man who turns back from righteousness to sin—the Lord will prepare him for the sword!
text.KJV: 26:28 There be two things that grieue my heart: and the third maketh me angry: a man of warre that suffereth pouerty, and men of vnderstanding that are not set by: and one that returneth from righteousnesse to sinne: the Lord prepareth such a one for the sword.
text.DRV: 26:28 Two sorts have appeared unto me hard and dangerous, a merchant is hardly rid of negligence: and a viteler shall not be justified from the sins of the lips.

text.Base: A merchant can hardly keep from wrongdoing, nor is a tradesman innocent of sin.
text.KJV: 26:29 A merchant shall hardly keepe himselfe from doing wrong: and an huckster shall not be freed from sinne.

text.Base: Many have sinned for the sake of profit, and one who seeks to grow rich must turn away his eyes.
text.KJV: 27:1 Many haue sinned for a smal matter: & he that seeketh for abundance will turne his eies away.
text.DRV: 27:1 Through poverty many have offended: and he that seeketh to be made rich, turneth away his eye.
text.NABRE: 27:1 Chapter 27 - For the sake of profit many sin, and the struggle for wealth blinds the eyes.

text.Base: As a stake is driven firmly into a fissure between stones, so sin is wedged in between selling and buying.
text.KJV: 27:2 As a naile sticketh fast betweene the ioynings of the stones: so doth sinne sticke close betweene buying and selling.
text.DRV: 27:2 As a stake is fastened in the midst of stones compact together, so also in the midst of selling and buying, sin shall be straitened.
text.NABRE: 27:2 A stake will be driven between fitted stones— sin will be wedged in between buying and selling.

text.Base: Unless a person holds zealously to the fear of the Lord, his house will be quickly overthrown.
text.KJV: 27:3 Unlesse a man hold himselfe diligently in the feare of the Lord, his house shall soone be ouerthrowen.
text.DRV: 27:3 Sin shall be destroyed with the sinner.
text.NABRE: 27:3 Unless one holds fast to the fear of the Lord, with sudden swiftness will one’s house be thrown down.

text.Base: When a sieve is shaken, the refuse appears; so do a person’s faults when he speaks.
text.KJV: 27:4 As when one sifteth with a sieue, the refuse remaineth, so the filth of man in his talke.
text.DRV: 27:4 If thou hold not thyself instantly in the fear of our Lord, thy house shall quickly be subverted.
text.NABRE: 27:4 When a sieve is shaken, the husks appear; so do people’s faults when they speak.

text.Base: The kiln tests the potter’s vessels; so the test of a person is in his conversation.
text.KJV: 27:5 The furnace prooueth the potters vessell: so the triall of man is in his reasoning.
text.DRV: 27:5 As in the shaking of a sieve the dust will remain: so the perplexity of a man in his cogitation.
text.NABRE: 27:5 The furnace tests the potter’s vessels; the test of a person is in conversation.

text.Base: Its fruit discloses the cultivation of a tree; so a person’s speech discloses the cultivation of his mind.
text.KJV: 27:6 The fruite declareth if the tree haue beene dressed: so is the vtterance of a conceit in the heart of man.
text.DRV: 27:6 The furnace trieth the potter's vessels, and the tentation of tribulation just men.
text.NABRE: 27:6 The fruit of a tree shows the care it has had; so speech discloses the bent of a person’s heart.

text.Base: One who discloses secrets destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.KJV: 27:16 Who so discouereth secrets, looseth his credit: and shall neuer find friend to his minde.
text.DRV: 27:16 Shedding of blood is in the brawling of the proud: and their cursing is a grievous hearing.
text.NABRE: 27:16 Whoever betrays a secret destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.

text.Base: Love your friend and keep faith with him; but if you disclose his secrets, do not follow after him.
text.KJV: 27:17 Loue thy friend, and be faithfull vnto him: but if thou bewrayest his secrets, follow no more after him.
text.DRV: 27:17 He that discloseth the secret of a friend loseth credit, and he shall not find a friend to his mind.
text.NABRE: 27:17 Cherish your friend, keep faith with him; but if you betray his secrets, do not go after him;

text.Base: Whoever winks with the eye plots mischief, and those who know him will keep their distance.
text.KJV: 27:22 He that winketh with the eies worketh euil, and he that knoweth him will depart from him.
text.DRV: 27:22 Follow him not, because he is far absent, for he is fled, as a doe out of the snare: because his soul is wounded.
text.NABRE: 27:22 Malice, Anger and Vengeance - Whoever has shifty eyes plots mischief and those who know him will keep their distance;

text.Base: The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance, for he keeps a strict account of their sins.
text.KJV: 28:1 He that reuengeth shall find vengeance from the Lord, and he will surely keepe his sinnes (in remembrance.)
text.DRV: 28:1 He that will be revenged, shall find revenge of our Lord, and keeping he will keep his sin.
text.NABRE: 28:1 Chapter 28 - The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance; indeed he remembers their sins in detail.

text.Base: Forgive your neighbor the wrong he has done, and then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.
text.KJV: 28:2 Forgiue thy neighbour the hurt that he hath done vnto thee, so shall thy sinnes also be forgiuen when thou prayest.
text.DRV: 28:2 Forgive thy neighbour hurting thee: & then shall thy sins be loosed to thee when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: 28:2 Forgive your neighbor the wrong done to you; then when you pray, your own sins will be forgiven.

text.Base: Does anyone harbor anger against another, and expect healing from the Lord?
text.KJV: 28:3 One man beareth hatred against another, and doeth he seeke pardon from the Lord?
text.DRV: 28:3 Man to man reserveth anger, and doth he seek remedy of God?
text.NABRE: 28:3 Does anyone nourish anger against another and expect healing from the LORD?

text.Base: If one has no mercy toward another like himself, can he then beg for pardon of his own sins?
text.KJV: 28:4 Hee sheweth no mercy to a man, which is like himselfe: and doeth hee aske forgiuenesse of his owne sinnes?
text.DRV: 28:4 He hath not mercy on a man like unto himself, and doth he entreat for his own sins?
text.NABRE: 28:4 Can one refuse mercy to a sinner like oneself, yet seek pardon for one’s own sins?

text.Base: Remember the end of your life, and set enmity aside; remember corruption and death, and be true to the commandments.
text.KJV: 28:6 Remember thy end, and let enimitie cease, corruption and death, and abide in the Commandements.
text.DRV: 28:6 Remember the last things, & cease to be at enmity:
text.NABRE: 28:6 Remember your last days and set enmity aside; remember death and decay, and cease from sin!

text.Base: If you blow on a spark, it will glow; if you spit on it, it will be put out; yet both come out of your mouth.
text.KJV: 28:12 If thou blow the sparke, it shall burne: if thou spit vpon it, it shall bee quenched, and both these come out of thy mouth.
text.DRV: 28:12 For according to the wood of the forest, so the fire burneth: and according to the power of a man, so shall his anger be, and according to his substance he will increase his anger.
text.NABRE: 28:12 The Evil Tongue - If you blow on a spark, it turns into flame, if you spit on it, it dies out; yet both you do with your mouth!

text.Base: Curse the whisperer and the double-tongued, for they have destroyed many who were at peace.
text.KJV: 28:13 Curse the whisperer, and double tongued: for such haue destroyed many that were at peace.
text.DRV: 28:13 Hasty contention kindleth a fire: & hasty strife sheddeth blood: and an ill testifying tongue bringeth death.
text.NABRE: 28:13 Cursed be gossips and the double-tongued, for they destroy the peace of many.

text.Base: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as have fallen because of the tongue.
text.KJV: 28:18 Many haue fallen by the edge of the sword: but not so many as haue fallen by the tongue.
text.DRV: 28:18 It hath cut the forces of peoples, and undone strong nations.
text.NABRE: 28:18 Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as by the tongue.

text.Base: The merciful lend to their neighbors; by holding out a helping hand they keep the commandments.
text.KJV: 29:1 Hee that is mercifull, will lende vnto his neighbour, and hee that strengthneth his hande, keepeth the Commandements.
text.DRV: 29:1 He that lendeth to his neighbour, doth mercy: and he that prevaileth with hand, keepeth the commandments.
text.NABRE: 29:1 Chapter 29 Loans, Alms and Surety - The merciful lend to their neighbor, by holding out a helping hand, they keep the commandments.

text.Base: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need; pay your neighbor back at the appointed time.
text.KJV: 29:2 Lend to thy neighbour in time of his need, and pay thou thy neighbour againe in due season.
text.DRV: 29:2 Lend to thy neighbour in the time of his necessity, and again repay thy neighbour in his time.
text.NABRE: 29:2 Lend to your neighbor in his time of need, and pay back your neighbor in time.

text.Base: Be patient with someone in humble circumstances, and do not keep him waiting for your alms.
text.KJV: 29:8 Yet haue thou patience with a man in poore estate, and delay not to shew him mercy.
text.DRV: 29:8 but if not, he will defraud him of his money, and possess him an enemy without cause:
text.NABRE: 29:8 But with those in humble circumstances be patient; do not keep them waiting for your alms.

text.Base: Lose your silver for the sake of a brother or a friend, and do not let it rust under a stone and be lost.
text.KJV: 29:10 Lose thy money for thy brother and thy friend, and let it not rust vnder a stone to be lost.
text.DRV: 29:10 Many have not lent, not because of wickedness, but they were afraid to be defrauded without cause.
text.NABRE: 29:10 Lose your money for relative or friend; do not hide it under a stone to rot.

text.Base: Lay up your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and it will profit you more than gold.
text.KJV: 29:11 Lay vp thy treasure according to the commandements of the most high, and it shall bring thee more profite then golde.
text.DRV: 29:11 But yet upon the humble be stronger of mind, & for alms defer him not.
text.NABRE: 29:11 Dispose of your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and that will profit you more than the gold.

text.Base: The essentials of life are water and bread and clothing, and a house to provide privacy.
text.KJV: 29:21 The chiefe thing for life is water and bread, and clothing, and an house to couer shame.
text.DRV: 29:21 The sinner and unclean person flyeth from his surety.
text.NABRE: 29:21 Frugality and Its Rewards - Life’s prime needs are water, bread, and clothing, and also a house for decent privacy.

text.Base: Better is the life of a poor person under the shelter of his own roof than sumptuous food in another’s house.
text.KJV: 29:22 Better is the life of a poore man in a meane cottage, then delicate fare in another mans house.
text.DRV: 29:22 Asinner counteth the goods of his surety to himself: and unthankful in mind, will forsake him that delivered him.
text.NABRE: 29:22 Better is the life of the poor under the shadow of their own roof than sumptuous banquets among strangers.

text.Base: To a sensible person these things are intolerable: expression of grievance from a household, and the insults of a moneylender.
text.KJV: 29:28 These things are grieuous to a man of vnderstanding: the vpbraiding of house-roome, and reproching of the lender.
text.DRV: 29:28 The beginning of man's life water & bread, and garment, and house covering his turpitude.
text.NABRE: 29:28 Painful things to a sensitive person are rebuke as a parasite and insults from creditors.

text.Base: He who loves his son will whip him often, so that he may rejoice at the way he turns out.
text.KJV: 30:1 Hee that loueth his sonne, causeth him oft to feele the rodde, that hee may haue ioy of him in the end.
text.DRV: 30:1 He that loveth his son, doth accustom him to stripes, that he may rejoice in his later end, and not grope after the doors of his neighbours.
text.NABRE: 30:1 Chapter 30 The Training of Children - Whoever loves a son will chastise him often, that he may be his joy when he grows up.

text.Base: He who disciplines his son will profit by him, and will boast of him among acquaintances.
text.KJV: 30:2 He that chastiseth his sonne, shall haue ioy in him, and shall reioyce of him among his acquaintance.
text.DRV: 30:2 He that teacheth his son, shall be praised in him, & in the midst of them of his household he shall glory in him.
text.NABRE: 30:2 Whoever disciplines a son will benefit from him, and boast of him among acquaintances.

text.Base: A horse untamed grows stubborn, and a son left to himself grows headstrong.
text.KJV: 30:8 An horse not broken becommeth headstrong: and a childe left to himselfe will be wilfull.
text.DRV: 30:8 An untamed horse becometh stubborn, and a dissolute child will become heady.
text.NABRE: 30:8 An untamed horse turns out stubborn; and a son left to himself grows up unruly.

text.Base: Discipline your son and make his yoke heavy, so that you may not be offended by his shamelessness.
text.KJV: 30:13 Chastise thy sonne, and hold him to labour, lest his lewd behauiour be an offence vnto thee.
text.DRV: 30:13 Teach thy son, and work in him, that thou offend not in his dishonesty.
text.NABRE: 30:13 Discipline your son and make heavy his yoke, lest you be offended by his shamelessness.

text.Base: Better off is a poor man who is well and strong in constitution than a rich man who is severely afflicted in body.
text.KJV: 30:14 Better is the poore being sound and strong of constitution, then a rich man that is afflicted in his body.
text.DRV: 30:14 Better is a poor man whole and strong of force, than a rich man weak and scourged with misery.
text.NABRE: 30:14 Health and Cheerfulness - Better the poor in vigorous health than the rich with bodily ills.

text.Base: Health and vigor are better than any gold, and a robust body than countless riches.
text.KJV: 30:15 Health and good state of body are aboue all gold, and a strong body aboue infinite wealth.
text.DRV: 30:15 The health of the soul in holiness of justice, is better than all gold and silver: and a sound body, than infinite revenues.
text.NABRE: 30:15 I would rather have bodily health than any gold, and contentment of spirit than pearls.

text.Base: Death is better than a miserable life, and eternal rest than chronic sickness.
text.KJV: 30:17 Death is better then a bitter life, or continuall sickenesse.
text.DRV: 30:17 Better is death than a bitter life: and everlasting rest, than continual sickness.
text.NABRE: 30:17 Better is death than a wretched life, everlasting sleep than constant illness.

text.Base: Do not give yourself over to sorrow, and do not distress yourself deliberately.
text.KJV: 30:21 Giue not ouer thy mind to heauinesse, and afflict not thy selfe in thine owne counsell.
text.DRV: 30:21 seeing with his eyes, & groaning, as an eunuch embracing a virgin and sighing.
text.NABRE: 30:21 Do not give in to sadness, or torment yourself deliberately.

text.Base: Beguile yourself and comfort your heart, and drive sorrow far from you, for sorrow has destroyed many, and there is no profit in it.
text.KJV: 30:23 Loue thine owne soule, and comfort thy heart, remoue sorrow far from thee: for sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit therein.
text.DRV: 30:23 Joyfulness of the heart, this is the life of a man, and a treasure without defect of holiness: and the joy of a man is long life.
text.NABRE: 30:23 Distract yourself and renew your courage, drive resentment far away from you; For grief has killed many, and nothing is to be gained from resentment.

text.Base: Jealousy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.KJV: 30:24 Enuie and wrath shorten the life, and carefulnesse bringeth age before the time.
text.DRV: 30:24 Have mercy on thine own soul, pleasing God, and refrain: and comfort thy heart in his holiness: and expel sorrow far from thee.
text.NABRE: 30:24 Envy and anger shorten one’s days, and anxiety brings on premature old age.

text.Base: Wakefulness over wealth wastes the flesh, and anxiety about it drives away sleep.
text.KJV: 31:1 Watching for riches, consumeth the flesh, and the care therof driueth away sleepe.
text.DRV: 31:1 Watching after honesty shall pine the flesh, & the thought thereof taketh away sleep.
text.NABRE: 31:1 Chapter 31 The Proper Attitude Toward Riches - Wakefulness over wealth wastes away the flesh, and anxiety over it drives away sleep.

text.Base: Are you seated at a great table? Do not be greedy at it, and do not say, “How much food there is here!”
text.KJV: 31:12 If thou sit at a bountifull table, bee not greedy vpon it, and say not, There is much meate on it.
text.DRV: 31:12 Art thou set at a great table? Open not thy jaw thereupon first.
text.NABRE: 31:12 Table Etiquette - Are you seated at the table of the great? Bring to it no greedy gullet, Nor say, “How much food there is here!”

text.Base: Remember that a greedy eye is a bad thing; what has been created more greedy than an eye? Therefore it sheds tears from every face.
text.KJV: 31:13 Remember that a wicked eye is an euill thing: and what is created more wicked then an eye? therefore it weepeth vpon euery occasion.
text.DRV: 31:13 Say not this: There be many things which are upon it.
text.NABRE: 31:13 Remember that the greedy eye is evil. What has been created more greedy than the eye? Therefore, it weeps for any cause.

text.Base: Eat like a human being what is set before you, and do not chew greedily, or you will be despised.
text.KJV: 31:16 Eate as it becommeth a man those things which are set before thee: and deuoure not, lest thou be hated.
text.DRV: 31:16 stretch not out thy hand first, & so contaminated with envy thou be ashamed.
text.NABRE: 31:16 Eat, like anyone else, what is set before you, but do not eat greedily, lest you be despised.

text.Base: Be the first to stop, as befits good manners, and do not be insatiable, or you will give offense.
text.KJV: 31:17 Leaue off first for maners sake, and be not vnsatiable, lest thou offend.
text.DRV: 31:17 Be not oppressed in a feast.
text.NABRE: 31:17 Be the first to stop, as befits good manners; and do not gorge yourself, lest you give offense.

text.Base: Do not try to prove your strength by wine-drinking, for wine has destroyed many.
text.KJV: 31:25 Shew not thy valiantnesse in wine, for wine hath destroyed many.
text.DRV: 31:25 And if thou hast been forced with eating much, rise from the midst, and vomit, and it shall refresh thee, and thou shalt not bring infirmity to thy body.
text.NABRE: 31:25 Let not wine be the proof of your strength, for wine has been the ruin of many.

text.Base: Wine is very life to human beings if drunk in moderation. What is life to one who is without wine? It has been created to make people happy.
text.KJV: 31:27 Wine is as good as life to a man if it be drunke moderatly: what life is then to a man that is without wine? for it was made to make men glad.
text.DRV: 31:27 In all thy works be quick, and all infirmity shall not chance unto thee.
text.NABRE: 31:27 Wine is very life to anyone, if taken in moderation. Does anyone really live who lacks the wine which from the beginning was created for joy?

text.Base: Wine drunk at the proper time and in moderation is rejoicing of heart and gladness of soul.
text.KJV: 31:28 Wine measurably drunke, and in season, bringeth gladnesse of the heart and cheerefulnesse of the minde.
text.DRV: 31:28 The lips of many shall bless him that is magnifical in breads, and the testimony of his truth is faithful.
text.NABRE: 31:28 Joy of heart, good cheer, and delight is wine enough, drunk at the proper time.

text.Base: Drunkenness increases the anger of a fool to his own injury, reducing his strength and adding wounds.
text.KJV: 31:30 Drunkennesse increaseth the rage of a foole till he offend, it diminisheth strength, and maketh wounds.
text.DRV: 31:30 Provoke not them that love wine: for wine hath destroyed very many.
text.NABRE: 31:30 Wine in excess is a snare for the fool; it lessens strength and multiplies wounds.

text.Base: If they make you master of the feast, do not promote yourself; be among them as one of them.
text.KJV: 32:1 If thou be made the master (of the feast) lift not thy selfe vp, but bee among them as one of the rest, take diligent care for them, and so sit downe.
text.DRV: 32:1 Have they made thee Ruler? Be not extolled: be among them as one of them.
text.NABRE: 32:1 Chapter 32 - If you are chosen to preside at a dinner, do not be puffed up, but with the guests be as one of them; Take care of them first and then sit down;

text.Base: When you have performed all your duties, take your place, so that you may be merry along with them and receive a wreath for your efficiency.
text.KJV: 32:2 And when thou hast done all thy office, take thy place that thou mayest be merry with them, and receiue a crowne for thy well ordering of the feast.
text.DRV: 32:2 Have care of them, and so sit thou still, and all thy care being dispatched, repose.
text.NABRE: 32:2 see to their needs, and then take your place, To share in their joy and receive a wreath for a job well done.

text.Base: Speak, you who are older, for it is your right, but with accurate knowledge, and do not interrupt the music.
text.KJV: 32:3 Speake thou that art the elder, for it becometh thee, but with sound iudgement, and hinder not musicke.
text.DRV: 32:3 That thou mayst rejoice for their sakes, & receive a crown as an ornament of grace, and obtain the dignity of the contribution.
text.NABRE: 32:3 You who are older, it is your right to speak, but temper your knowledge and do not interrupt the singing.

text.Base: Speak, young man, if you are indispensable, but no more than twice, and only if asked.
text.KJV: 32:7 Speake yong man, if there be need of thee: and yet scarsely when thou art twise asked:
text.DRV: 32:7 Alittle pearl of the carbuncle in an ornament of gold, and the comparison of musicians in a banquet of wine.
text.NABRE: 32:7 Speak, young man, only when necessary, when they have asked you more than once.

text.Base: The one who seeks God will accept his discipline, and those who rise early to seek him will find favor.
text.KJV: 32:14 Who so feareth the Lord, will receiue his discipline, and they that seeke him early, shall find fauour.
text.DRV: 32:14 before hail there shall go lightning: & grace shall go before shamefastness, & for thy reverence good grace shall come to thee.
text.NABRE: 32:14 The Providence of God - Whoever seeks God must accept discipline; and whoever resorts to him obtains an answer.

text.Base: In all that you do trust your own soul, for this is the keeping of the commandments.
text.KJV: 32:23 In euery good worke trust thy owne soule: for this is the keeping of the commandements.
text.DRV: 32:23 Yea after he hath done with fear without counsel, he shall be controlled even by his own pursuits.
text.NABRE: 32:23 Whatever you do, be on your guard, for whoever does so keeps the commandments.

text.Base: No evil will befall the one who fears the Lord, but in trials such a one will be delivered again and again.
text.KJV: 33:1 There shall no euill happen vnto him that feareth the Lord, but in temptation euen againe he will deliuer him.
text.DRV: 33:1 To him that feareth our Lord evils shall not happen, but in tentation God will keep him, and deliver him from evils.
text.NABRE: 33:1 Chapter 33 - No evil can harm the one who fears the LORD; through trials, again and again he is rescued.

text.Base: The wise person will not hate the law, but the one who is hypocritical about it is like a boat in a storm.
text.KJV: 33:2 A wise man hateth not the Law, but he that is an hypocrite therein, is as a ship in a storme.
text.DRV: 33:2 Awise man hateth not the commandments and justices, and he shall not be shaken as a ship in a storm.
text.NABRE: 33:2 Whoever hates the law is without wisdom, and is tossed about like a boat in a storm.

text.Base: Why is one day more important than another, when all the daylight in the year is from the sun?
text.KJV: 33:7 Why doth one day excell another? when as all the light of euery day in the yeere is of the Sunne.
text.DRV: 33:7 Why doth one day excel another, and one light another, and one year another year of the sun?
text.NABRE: 33:7 Why is one day more important than another, when the same sun lights up every day of the year?

text.Base: By the Lord’s knowledge they were kept distinct, and he appointed the different seasons and festivals.
text.KJV: 33:8 By the knowledge of the Lord they were distinguished: and he altered seasons and feasts.
text.DRV: 33:8 By the knowledge of our Lord they were separated, the sun being made, and keeping the precept.
text.NABRE: 33:8 By the LORD’s knowledge they are kept distinct; and he designates the seasons and feasts.

text.Base: All human beings come from the ground, and humankind was created out of the dust.
text.KJV: 33:10 And all men are from the ground, and Adam was created of earth.
text.DRV: 33:10 Of them God exalted and magnified, and of them he put into the number of days. And all men are of the ground, and of the earth, from whence Adam was created.
text.NABRE: 33:10 Likewise, all people are of clay, and from earth humankind was formed;

text.Base: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them and made their ways diverse.
text.KJV: 33:11 In much knowledge the Lord hath diuided them, and made their wayes diuers.
text.DRV: 33:11 In the multitude of the discipline of our Lord he separated them, and changed their ways.
text.NABRE: 33:11 In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them, and he designated their different ways.

text.Base: Like clay in the hand of the potter, to be molded just as he pleases, so all are in the hand of their Maker, to be given whatever he decides.
text.KJV: 33:13 As the clay is in the potters hand to fashion it at his pleasure: so man is in the hand of him that made him, to render to them as liketh him best.
text.DRV: 33:13 As potter's clay is in his hand, to fashion and dispose it.
text.NABRE: 33:13 Like clay in the hands of a potter, to be molded according to his pleasure, So are people in the hands of their Maker, to be dealt with as he decides.

text.Base: Fodder and a stick and burdens for a donkey; bread and discipline and work for a slave.
text.KJV: 33:24 Fodder, a wand, and burdens, are for the asse: and bread, correction, and worke for a seruant.
text.DRV: 33:24 Give no stain to thy glory. In the day of the consummation of the days of thy life, and in the time of thy decease distribute thine inheritance.
text.NABRE: 33:24 When your few days reach their limit, at the time of death distribute your inheritance.

text.Base: Put him to work, so that he may not be idle, for idleness teaches much evil.
text.KJV: 33:27 Sende him to labour that hee be not idle: for idlenesse teacheth much euill.
text.DRV: 33:27 The yoke and the rein bend a stiff neck, and continual works do bow a servant.
text.NABRE: 33:27 The yoke and harness will bow the neck; and for a wicked slave, punishment in the stocks.

text.Base: The senseless have vain and false hopes, and dreams give wings to fools.
text.KJV: 34:1 The hopes of a man voyd of vnderstanding are vaine, and false: and dreames lift vp fooles.
text.DRV: 34:1 Vain hope & lying is to a foolish man: & dreams extol the unwise.
text.NABRE: 34:1 Chapter 34 Trust in the Lord and Not in Dreams - Empty and false are the hopes of the senseless, and dreams give wings to fools.

text.Base: As one who catches at a shadow and pursues the wind, so is anyone who believes in dreams.
text.KJV: 34:2 Who so regardeth dreames, is like him that catcheth at a shadow, and followeth after the winde.
text.DRV: 34:2 As he that apprehendeth a shadow, and pursueth the wind: so is he also that attendeth to lying visions.
text.NABRE: 34:2 Like one grasping at shadows or chasing the wind, so anyone who believes in dreams.

text.Base: The one who fears the Lord will not be timid, or play the coward, for the Lord is his hope.
text.KJV: 34:14 Who so feareth the Lord, shall not feare nor be afraid, for hee is his hope.
text.DRV: 34:14 The spirit of them that fear God is sought, & at his sight shall be blessed.
text.NABRE: 34:14 Living is the spirit of those who fear the Lord,

text.Base: Happy is the soul of the one who fears the Lord! To whom does he look? And who is his support?
text.KJV: 34:15 Blessed is the soule of him that feareth the Lord: to whom doeth hee looke? and who is his strength?
text.DRV: 34:15 For their hope is on him that saveth them, and the eyes of God upon them that love him.
text.NABRE: 34:15 for their hope is in their savior.

text.Base: If one sacrifices from what has been wrongfully acquired, the offering is blemished; the gifts of the lawless are not acceptable.
