text.KJV: 34:18 Hee that sacrificeth of a thing wrongfully gotten, his offering is ridiculous, and the giftes of vniust men are not accepted.
text.DRV: 34:18 To whom doth he look, and who is his strength?
text.NABRE: 34:18 In whom does he trust, and who is his support?

text.Base: The Most High is not pleased with the offerings of the ungodly, nor for a multitude of sacrifices does he forgive sins.
text.KJV: 34:19 The most high is not pleased with the offerings of the wicked, neither is he pacified for sinne by the multitude of sacrifices.
text.DRV: 34:19 The eyes of our Lord are upon them that fear him, a protector of might, a stay of strength, a cover from the heat, and shadow for the noon time,
text.NABRE: 34:19 The eyes of the Lord are upon those who love him; he is their mighty shield and strong support, A shelter from the heat, a shade from the noonday sun, a guard against stumbling, a help against falling.

text.Base: If one washes after touching a corpse, and touches it again, what has been gained by washing?
text.KJV: 34:25 He that washeth himselfe after the touching of a dead body, if he touch it againe, what auaileth his washing?
text.DRV: 34:25 The bread of the needy, is the life of the poor: he that defraudeth it, is a man of blood.
text.NABRE: 34:25 The bread of charity is life itself for the needy; whoever withholds it is a murderer.

text.Base: So if a person fasts for his sins, and goes again and does the same things, who will listen to his prayer? And what has he gained by humbling himself?
text.KJV: 34:26 So is it with a man that fasteth for his sinnes, and goeth againe and doeth the same: who will heare his prayer, or what doeth his humbling profit him?
text.DRV: 34:26 He that taketh away bread in sweat, is as he that killeth his neighbour.
text.NABRE: 34:26 To take away a neighbor’s living is to commit murder;

text.Base: The one who keeps the law makes many offerings; one who heeds the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.
text.KJV: 35:1 Hee that keepeth the law, bringeth offerings enow: he that taketh heed to the commandement, offereth a peace offering.
text.DRV: 35:1 He that keepeth the law, multiplieth oblation.
text.NABRE: 35:1 Chapter 35 - To keep the law is to make many offerings;

text.Base: The one who returns a kindness offers choice flour, and one who gives alms sacrifices a thank offering.
text.KJV: 35:2 He that requiteth a good turne, offereth fine floure: and he that giueth almes, sacrificeth praise.
text.DRV: 35:2 It is an wholesome sacrifice to attend to the commandments, and to depart from all iniquity.
text.NABRE: 35:2 whoever observes the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.

text.Base: To keep from wickedness is pleasing to the Lord, and to forsake unrighteousness is an atonement.
text.KJV: 35:3 To depart from wickednesse is a thing pleasing to the Lord: and to forsake vnrighteousnesse, is a propitiation.
text.DRV: 35:3 And to offer propitiation by sacrifice for injustices, and to retire from injustice is prayer for sins.
text.NABRE: 35:3 By works of charity one offers fine flour,

text.Base: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,
text.KJV: 35:4 Thou shalt not appeare emptie before the Lord:
text.DRV: 35:4 He who offereth the flour of wheat, shall give thanks: and he that is merciful offereth sacrifice.
text.NABRE: 35:4 and one who gives alms presents a sacrifice of praise.

text.Base: for all these things are to be done because of the commandment.
text.KJV: 35:5 For all these things because of the commandement.
text.DRV: 35:5 To depart from iniquity is a thing that pleaseth our Lord well, and to depart from injustice is an entreating for sins.
text.NABRE: 35:5 To refrain from evil pleases the Lord, and to avoid injustice is atonement.

text.Base: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, and as generously as you can afford.
text.KJV: 35:10 Giue vnto the most high, according as hee hath enriched thee, and as thou hast gotten, giue with a cheerefull eye.
text.DRV: 35:10 Render glory to God with a good mind: and diminish not the first fruits of thine hands.
text.NABRE: 35:10 With a generous spirit pay homage to the Lord, and do not spare your freewill gifts.

text.Base: For the Lord is the one who repays, and he will repay you sevenfold.
text.KJV: 35:11 For the Lord recompenseth, and will giue thee seuen times as much.
text.DRV: 35:11 In every gift make thy countenance cheerful, and in joyfulness sanctify thy tithes.
text.NABRE: 35:11 With each contribution show a cheerful countenance, and pay your tithes in a spirit of joy.

text.Base: Do not the tears of the widow run down her cheek as she cries out against the one who causes them to fall?
text.KJV: 35:15 Doeth not the teares run downe the widowes cheeks? and is not her crie against him that causeth them to fall?
text.DRV: 35:15 And look not upon an unjust sacrifice, because our Lord is judge, and there is not with him the glory of person.
text.NABRE: 35:15 Do not trust in sacrifice of the fruits of extortion, For he is a God of justice, who shows no partiality.

text.Base: The prayer of the humble pierces the clouds, and it will not rest until it reaches its goal.
text.KJV: 35:17 The prayer of the humble pierceth the clouds: and till it come nigh he will not be comforted: and will not depart till the most High shall beholde to iudge righteously, and execute iudgement.
text.DRV: 35:17 He will not despise the prayers of the pupil: nor the widow, if she pour out speech of mourning.
text.NABRE: 35:17 He does not forsake the cry of the orphan, nor the widow when she pours out her complaint.

text.Base: Have mercy upon us, O Lord, the God of all, and look upon us,
text.KJV: 36:1 Haue mercie vpon vs, O Lord God of all, and behold vs:
text.DRV: 36:1 Have mercy upon us o God of all, and respect us, and shew us the light of thy mercies:
text.NABRE: 36:1 Chapter 36 A Prayer for God’s People - Come to our aid, O God of the universe,

text.Base: and put the fear of you upon all the nations.
text.KJV: 36:2 And send thy feare vpon all the nations that seeke not after thee.
text.DRV: 36:2 and send in thy fear upon the nations, that have not sought after thee, that they may know that there is no God but thou, and that they may shew forth thy glorious things.
text.NABRE: 36:2 and put all the nations in dread of you!

text.Base: Reward those who wait for you, and let your prophets be found trustworthy.
text.KJV: 36:16 Reward them that wait for thee, and let thy prophets be found faithfull.
text.DRV: 36:16 Replenish Sion with thy words that cannot be uttered, & thy people with thy glory.

text.Base: A woman will accept any man, but one girl is better than another.
text.KJV: 36:21 A woman will receiue euery man, yet is one daughter better then another
text.DRV: 36:21 The jaws taste venison, & the wise heart lying words,

text.Base: A woman’s beauty gladdens the countenance, and a man desires nothing more.
text.KJV: 36:22 The beautie of a woman cheareth the countenance, and a man loueth nothing better.
text.DRV: 36:22 Aperverse heart will give sorrow, and a cunning man will resist it.

text.Base: The man who acquires a wife gets his best possession, a helper fit for him and a pillar of support.
text.KJV: 36:24 He that getteth a wife, beginneth a possession, a helpe like vnto himselfe, and a pillar of rest.
text.DRV: 36:24 The beauty of a woman cheereth the face of her husband, and increaseth the desire above all man's concupiscence.

text.Base: Where there is no fence, the property will be plundered; and where there is no wife, a man will become a fugitive and a wanderer.
text.KJV: 36:25 Where no hedge is, there the possession is spoiled: and he that hath no wife will wander vp and downe mourning.
text.DRV: 36:25 If there be a tongue of curing, there is also a mitigating and of mercy: her husband is not according to the sons of men.

text.Base: Every friend says, “I too am a friend”; but some friends are friends only in name.
text.KJV: 37:1 Euery friend saieth, I am his friend also: but there is a friend which is onely a friend in name.
text.DRV: 37:1 Every friend will say: I also have joined friendship: but there is a friend, in name only a friend. Doth there not sorrow remain even to death?
text.NABRE: 37:1 Chapter 37 - Every friend declares friendship, but there are friends who are friends in name only.

text.Base: Is it not a sorrow like that for death itself when a dear friend turns into an enemy?
text.KJV: 37:2 Is it not a griefe vnto death, when a companion and friend is turned to an enemie?
text.DRV: 37:2 But a companion and friend will be turned to enmity.
text.NABRE: 37:2 Is it not a sorrow unto death when your other self becomes your enemy?

text.Base: But stay constantly with a God-fearing person whom you know to keep the commandments,
text.KJV: 37:12 But be continually with a godly man, whom thou knowest to keepe the commandements of the Lord, whose minde is according to thy minde, and will sorrow with thee, if thou shalt miscarry.
text.DRV: 37:12 With an irreligious man treat not of holiness, & with the unjust of justice, and with a woman of the thing whereof she is jealous: with a fearful man of war, with a merchant of traffic, with a buyer of selling, with an envious man of giving thanks,
text.NABRE: 37:12 Instead, associate with a religious person, who you know keeps the commandments; Who is like-minded with yourself and will grieve for you if you fall.

text.Base: And heed the counsel of your own heart, for no one is more faithful to you than it is.
text.KJV: 37:13 And let the counsell of thine owne heart stand: for there is no man more faithfull vnto thee then it.
text.DRV: 37:13 with the impious of piety, with the unhonest of honesty, with the field labourer of all work,
text.NABRE: 37:13 Then, too, heed your own heart’s counsel; for there is nothing you can depend on more.

text.Base: For a person’s soul sometimes keeps him better informed than seven sentinels sitting high on a watchtower.
text.KJV: 37:14 For a mans minde is sometime wont to tell him more then seuen watchmen, that sit aboue in an high towre.
text.DRV: 37:14 with him that worketh by the year of the ending of the year, with a slothful servant of much working: attend not to these in all counsel.
text.NABRE: 37:14 The heart can reveal your situation better than seven sentinels on a tower.

text.Base: My child, test your soul while you live; see what is bad for it and do not give it that.
text.KJV: 37:27 My sonne prooue thy soule in thy life, and see what is euill for it, and giue not that vnto it.
text.DRV: 37:27 Awise man shall be filled with blessings, and they that see will praise him.

text.Base: For not everything is good for everyone, and no one enjoys everything.
text.KJV: 37:28 For all things are not profitable for all men, neither hath euery soule pleasure in euery thing.
text.DRV: 37:28 The life of a man is in the number of days: but the days of Israel are innumerable.

text.Base: For overeating brings sickness, and gluttony leads to nausea.
text.KJV: 37:30 For excesse of meates, bringeth sicknesse, and surfetting will turne into choler.
text.DRV: 37:30 Son in thy life prove thy soul: & if it be wicked, give it not power:

text.Base: Honor physicians for their services, for the Lord created them;
text.KJV: 38:1 Honour a Phisitian with the honour due vnto him, for the vses which you may haue of him: for the Lord hath created him.
text.DRV: 38:1 Honour the physician for necessity: for the Highest hath created him.
text.NABRE: 38:1 Chapter 38 Sickness and Death - Make friends with the doctor, for he is essential to you; God has also established him in his profession.

text.Base: for their gift of healing comes from the Most High, and they are rewarded by the king.
text.KJV: 38:2 For of the most High commeth healing, and he shall receiue honour of the King.
text.DRV: 38:2 For all medicine is of God, & it shall receive gift of the king.
text.NABRE: 38:2 From God the doctor has wisdom, and from the king he receives sustenance.

text.Base: The Lord created medicines out of the earth, and the sensible will not despise them.
text.KJV: 38:4 The Lord hath created medicines out of the earth; and he that is wise will not abhorre them.
text.DRV: 38:4 The Highest hath created medicines of the earth, and a wise man will not abhor them.
text.NABRE: 38:4 God makes the earth yield healing herbs which the prudent should not neglect;

text.Base: My child, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to the Lord, and he will heal you.
text.KJV: 38:9 My sonne, in thy sickenesse be not negligent: but pray vnto the Lord, and he will make thee whole.
text.DRV: 38:9 Son in thine infirmity contemn not thyself, but pray our Lord, and he will cure thee.
text.NABRE: 38:9 My son, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to God, for it is he who heals.

text.Base: Give up your faults and direct your hands rightly, and cleanse your heart from all sin.
text.KJV: 38:10 Leaue off from sinne, and order thy hands aright, and cleanse thy heart from all wickednesse.
text.DRV: 38:10 Turn away from sin, and direct thy hands, and from all offence cleanse thy heart.
text.NABRE: 38:10 Flee wickedness and purify your hands; cleanse your heart of every sin.

text.Base: Then give the physician his place, for the Lord created him; and do not let him leave you, for you need him.
text.KJV: 38:12 Then giue place to the phisitian, for the Lord hath created him: let him not go from thee, for thou hast need of him.
text.DRV: 38:12 For our Lord created him: and let him not depart from thee, because his works be necessary.
text.NABRE: 38:12 Then give the doctor his place lest he leave; you need him too,

text.Base: My child, let your tears fall for the dead, and as one who is suffering terribly begin the lament.
text.KJV: 38:16 My sonne, let teares fall downe ouer the dead, and begin to lament, as if thou hadst suffered great harme thy selfe: and then couer his body according to the custome, & neglect not his buriall.
text.DRV: 38:16 Son, upon the dead shed tears, and begin to weep as having suffered doleful things, and according to judgement cover his body, and neglect not his burial.
text.NABRE: 38:16 My son, shed tears for one who is dead with wailing and bitter lament; As is only proper, prepare the body, and do not absent yourself from the burial.

text.Base: Do not give your heart to sorrow; drive it away, and remember your own end.
text.KJV: 38:21 Forget it not, for there is no turning againe: thou shalt not doe him good, but hurt thy selfe.
text.DRV: 38:21 Give not thine heart into heaviness, but expel it from thee: and remember the latter ends,
text.NABRE: 38:21 Do not recall him, for there is no hope of his return; you do him no good, and you harm yourself.

text.Base: The wisdom of the scribe comes through opportunity of leisure; the one who has little business can become wise.
text.KJV: 38:24 The wisedome of a learned man cōmeth by opportunitie of leasure: & he that hath litle busines shal become wise.
text.DRV: 38:24 In the repose of the dead make the memory of him to rest, and comfort him in the departing of his spirit.
text.NABRE: 38:24 Vocations of the Skilled Worker and the Scribe - The scribe’s wisdom increases wisdom; whoever is free from toil can become wise.

text.Base: How different is the one who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He seeks out the wisdom of all the ancients, and is concerned with prophecies;
text.KJV: 39:1 But hee that giueth his minde to the Law of the most high, and is occupied in the meditation thereof, wil seeke out the wisdome of all the ancient, and be occupied in prophecies.
text.DRV: 39:1 The wise man will search out the wisdom of all the ancients, and will be occupied in the prophets.
text.NABRE: 39:1 Chapter 39 - How different the person who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He explores the wisdom of all the ancients and is occupied with the prophecies;

text.Base: he preserves the sayings of the famous and penetrates the subtleties of parables;
text.KJV: 39:2 Hee will keepe the sayings of the renowmed men: and where subtile parables are, he will be there also.
text.DRV: 39:2 He will keep the narration of famous men, and will enter withal into the subtilties of parables.
text.NABRE: 39:2 He preserves the discourses of the famous, and goes to the heart of involved sayings;

text.Base: If the great Lord is willing, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; he will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give thanks to the Lord.
text.KJV: 39:6 When the great Lord will, he shall bee filled with the spirit of vnderstanding: he shal powre out wise sentences, and giue thankes vnto the Lord in his prayer.
text.DRV: 39:6 He will give his heart to watch early unto our Lord, that made him, and he will pray in the sight of the Highest.
text.NABRE: 39:6 If it pleases the Lord Almighty, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; He will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give praise to the Lord.

text.Base: “All the works of the Lord are very good, and whatever he commands will be done at the appointed time.”
text.KJV: 39:16 Al the works of the Lord are exceeding good, & whatsoeuer hee commandeth, shalbe accomplished in due season.
text.DRV: 39:16 I will yet consult that I may declare: For as with fury I am replenished.
text.NABRE: 39:16 The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.

text.Base: Basic to all the needs of human life are water and fire and iron and salt and wheat flour and milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil and clothing.
text.KJV: 39:26 The principall things for the whole vse of mans life, are water, fire, yron, and salt, floure of wheate, honie, milke, and the blood of the grape, and oyle, and clothing.
text.DRV: 39:26 It is not to be said: What is this, or what is that? For all things shall be sought in their time.
text.NABRE: 39:26 Chief of all needs for human life are water and fire, iron and salt, The heart of the wheat, milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.

text.Base: All these are good for the godly, but for sinners they turn into evils.
text.KJV: 39:27 All these things are for good to the godly: so to the sinners they are turned into euill.
text.DRV: 39:27 His blessing hath overflowed as a stream.
text.NABRE: 39:27 For the good all these are good, but for the wicked they turn out evil.

text.Base: Hard work was created for everyone, and a heavy yoke is laid on the children of Adam, from the day they come forth from their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.KJV: 40:1 Great trauaile is created for euery man, and an heauy yoke is vpon the sons of Adam, from the day that they goe out of their mothers wombe, till the day that they returne to the mother of all things.
text.DRV: 40:1 Great travail is created to all men, and an heavy yoke upon the children of Adam, from the day of their coming forth of their mother's womb, until the day of their burying, into the mother of all.
text.NABRE: 40:1 Chapter 40 Joys and Miseries of Life - A great anxiety has God allotted, and a heavy yoke, to the children of Adam, From the day they leave their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.

text.Base: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from the waters returns to the sea.
text.KJV: 40:11 All things that are of the earth shal turne to the earth againe: and that which is of the waters doeth returne into the Sea.
text.DRV: 40:11 All things that are of the earth, shall turn into the earth, and all waters shall return into the sea.
text.NABRE: 40:11 All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from above returns above.

text.Base: Kindness is like a garden of blessings, and almsgiving endures forever.
text.KJV: 40:17 Bountifulnes is as a most fruitfull garden, and mercifulnesse endureth for euer.
text.DRV: 40:17 Grace is as paradise in blessings, and mercy remaineth for ever.
text.NABRE: 40:17 But goodness, like eternity, will never be cut off, and righteousness endures forever.

text.Base: To be self-sufficient and to be a worker—both are sweet, but better than both is the one who finds a treasure.
text.KJV: 40:18 To labour & to be content with that a man hath, is a sweet life: but hee that findeth a treasure, is aboue them both.
text.DRV: 40:18 The life of a workman that is sufficient for himself shall be sweet, and in it thou shalt find a treasure.
text.NABRE: 40:18 Wealth or wages can make life sweet, but better than either, finding a treasure.

text.Base: Wine and music gladden the heart, but the love of wisdom is better than both.
text.KJV: 40:20 Wine & musicke reioyce the heart: but the loue of wisedome is aboue them both.
text.DRV: 40:20 Wine and music make a joyful heart: and the love of wisdom is above both.
text.NABRE: 40:20 Wine and strong drink delight the soul, but better than either, love of friends.

text.Base: Flute and harp make pleasant melody, but a gentle tongue is better than both.
text.KJV: 40:21 The pipe and the psalterie make sweet melodie: but a pleasant tongue is aboue them both.
text.DRV: 40:21 Shalms, and Psaltery make sweet melody, and a sweet tongue is above both.
text.NABRE: 40:21 Flute and harp offer sweet melody, but better than either, a pure tongue.

text.Base: A friend and a companion encounter each other at the opportune moment, but better than both is a wife with her husband.
text.KJV: 40:23 A friend and companion neuer meet amisse: but aboue both is a wife with her husband.
text.DRV: 40:23 Afriend and companion meeting together in time, & above them both is a woman with her husband.
text.NABRE: 40:23 A friend and a neighbor are timely guides, but better than either, a sensible wife.

text.Base: O death, how bitter is the thought of you to the one who lives at peace among his possessions,
text.KJV: 41:1 O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that liueth at rest in his possessions, vnto the man that hath nothing to vexe him, and that hath prosperity in all things: yea vnto him that is yet able to receiue meate?
text.DRV: 41:1 O Death how bitter is thy memory to a man that hath peace in his riches:
text.NABRE: 41:1 Chapter 41 - O death! How bitter is the thought of you for the one at peace in his home, For the one who is serene and always successful, who can still enjoy life’s pleasures.

text.Base: O death, how welcome is your sentence to one who is in need and is failing in strength,
text.KJV: 41:2 O death, acceptable is thy sentence vnto the needy, and vnto him whose strength faileth, that is now in the last age, and is vexed with all things, and to him that despaireth and hath lost patience.
text.DRV: 41:2 to a man that is at rest, and whose ways are prosperous in all things, and that is yet able to take meat!
text.NABRE: 41:2 O death! How welcome is your sentence to the weak, failing in strength, Stumbling and tripping on everything, with sight gone and hope lost.

text.Base: Do not fear the sentence of death; remember those who went before you and those who will come after.
text.KJV: 41:3 Feare not the sentence of death, remember them that haue beene before thee, and that come after, for this is the sentence of the Lord ouer all flesh.
text.DRV: 41:3 O death, thy judgement is good to a needy man, and him that is diminished in strength,
text.NABRE: 41:3 Do not fear death’s decree for you; remember, it embraces those before you and those to come.

text.Base: Have regard for your name, since it will remain for you longer than a thousand great treasures of gold.
text.KJV: 41:12 Haue regard to thy name: for that shall continue with thee aboue a thousand great treasures of gold.
text.DRV: 41:12 And if ye be born, ye shall be born in malediction: and if ye die, in malediction shall be your portion.
text.NABRE: 41:12 Have respect for your name, for it will stand by you more than thousands of precious treasures.

text.Base: The days of a good life are numbered, but a good name lasts forever.
text.KJV: 41:13 A good life hath but few daies: but a good name endureth for euer.
text.DRV: 41:13 All things that are of the earth, shall return into the earth: so the impious from malediction to perdition.
text.NABRE: 41:13 The good things of life last a number of days, but a good name, for days without number.

text.Base: Of the following things do not be ashamed, and do not let partiality lead you to sin:
text.KJV: 42:1 Of these things be not thou ashamed, and accept no person to sinne thereby.
text.DRV: 42:1 Repeat not the word which thou hast heard, neither reveal thou of a secret word, & thou shalt indeed be without confusion, and shalt find grace in the sight of all men: be not ashamed for all these things, and accept not person thereby to sin.
text.NABRE: 42:1 Chapter 42 - Of repeating what you hear, of betraying any secret. Be ashamed of the right things, and you will find favor in the sight of all. But of these things do not be ashamed, lest you sin to save face:

text.Base: of the law of the Most High and his covenant, and of justice to acquit the ungodly;
text.KJV: 42:2 Of the Law of the most High, and his Couenant, and of iudgement to iustifie the vngodly:
text.DRV: 42:2 Of the law of the Highest, and his testament, and of judgement to justify the impious,
text.NABRE: 42:2 Of the Law of the Most High and his precepts, or of justice that acquits the ungodly;

text.Base: I will now call to mind the works of the Lord, and will declare what I have seen. By the words of the Lord his works are done.
text.KJV: 42:15 I will now remember the works of the Lord, and declare the things that I haue seene: in the words of the Lord are his workes.
text.DRV: 42:15 Itherefore will be mindful of the works of our Lord, and I will shew forth which I have seen. By the words of our Lord are his works.
text.NABRE: 42:15 The Works of God in Nature - Now will I recall God’s works; what I have seen, I will describe. By the LORD’s word his works were brought into being; he accepts the one who does his will.

text.Base: He searches out the abyss and the human heart; he understands their innermost secrets.
text.KJV: 42:18 He seeketh out the deepe and the heart, and considereth their crafty deuices: for the Lord knoweth all that may be knowen, and he beholdeth the signes of the world.
text.DRV: 42:18 He hath searched out the depth, and the heart of men: & in their subtility he hath considered.
text.NABRE: 42:18 He searches out the abyss and penetrates the heart; their secrets he understands. For the Most High possesses all knowledge, and sees from of old the things that are to come.

text.Base: All things are in pairs, one opposite the other, and he has made nothing incomplete.
text.KJV: 42:24 All things are double one against another: and hee hath made nothing vnperfit.
text.DRV: 42:24 All these live, and remain for ever, and in all necessity all things obey him.
text.NABRE: 42:24 All of them differ, one from another, yet none of them has he made in vain;

text.Base: The pride of the higher realms is the clear firmament, the appearance of heaven in a spectacle of glory.
text.KJV: 43:1 The pride of the height, the cleare firmament, the beautie of heauen, with his glorious shew;
text.DRV: 43:1 The firmament of height is his beauty, the beauty of heaven is in the vision of glory.
text.NABRE: 43:1 Chapter 43 - The beauty of the celestial height and the pure firmament, heaven itself manifests its glory.

text.Base: The sun, when it appears, proclaims as it rises what a marvelous instrument it is, the work of the Most High.
text.KJV: 43:2 The Sunne when it appeareth, declaring at his rising, a marueilous instrument, the worke of the most High.
text.DRV: 43:2 The sun in sight declaring at his coming forth, a marvelous instrument, the work of the Highest.
text.NABRE: 43:2 The sun at its rising shines at its fullest, a wonderful instrument, the work of the Most High!

text.Base: The moon also, at its correct time, is the guide of the seasons and an everlasting sign.
text.KJV: 43:6 He made the Moone also to serue in her season, for a declaration of times, and a signe of the world.
text.DRV: 43:6 And the moon in all in her time, is the shewing of season and the sign of age.
text.NABRE: 43:6 It is the moon that marks the changing seasons, governing the times, their lasting sign.

text.Base: Look at the rainbow, and praise him who made it; it is exceedingly beautiful in its brightness.
text.KJV: 43:11 Looke vpon the rainebow, and praise him that made it, very beautifull it is in the brightnesse thereof.
text.DRV: 43:11 In the words of the holy one they shall stand to judgement, and shall not fail in their watches.
text.NABRE: 43:11 Behold the rainbow! Then bless its Maker, for majestic indeed is its splendor;

text.Base: By his command he sends the driving snow and speeds the lightnings of his judgment.
text.KJV: 43:13 By his commandement hee maketh the snow to fall apace, and sendeth swiftly the lightnings of his iudgment.
text.DRV: 43:13 It hath compassed heaven in the circuit of his glory, the hands of the Highest have opened it.
text.NABRE: 43:13 His rebuke marks out the path for the hail, and makes the flashes of his judgment shine forth.

text.Base: We could say more but could never say enough; let the final word be, “He is the all.”
text.KJV: 43:27 We may speake much, & yet come short: wherefore in summe, he is all.
text.DRV: 43:27 There are goodly works, & marvelous: diverse kinds of beasts, & of all cattle, & the creature of mighty beasts.
text.NABRE: 43:27 More than this we need not add; let the last word be, he is the all!

text.Base: Exalt him as much as you can, for he will surpass even that; and when you exalt him, summon all your strength, and do not grow weary, for you can never exalt him enough.
text.KJV: 43:30 When you glorifie the Lord exalt him as much as you can: for euen yet wil he farre exceed, and when you exalt him, put foorth all your strength, and be not weary: for you can neuer goe farre enough.
text.DRV: 43:30 Glorifying him how far shall we be able? For the omnipotent himself is above all his works.
text.NABRE: 43:30 Lift up your voices to glorify the LORD as much as you can, for there is still more. Extol him with renewed strength, do not grow weary, for you cannot fathom him.

text.Base: Let us now sing the praises of famous men, our ancestors in their generations.
text.KJV: 44:1 Let vs now praise famous men, and our Fathers that begat vs.
text.DRV: 44:1 Let us praise glorious men, and our fathers in their generation.
text.NABRE: 44:1 Chapter 44 Praise of Israel’s Great Ancestors - I will now praise the godly, our ancestors, in their own time,

text.Base: The Lord apportioned to them great glory, his majesty from the beginning.
text.KJV: 44:2 The Lorde hath wrought great glory by them, through his great power from the beginning.
text.DRV: 44:2 Much glory hath our Lord made by his magnificence from the beginning of the world.
text.NABRE: 44:2 The abounding glory of the Most High’s portion, his own part, since the days of old.

text.Base: But of others there is no memory; they have perished as though they had never existed;
text.KJV: 44:9 And some there be, which haue no memorial, who are perished as though they had neuer bene, and are become as though they had neuer bene borne, and their children after them.
text.DRV: 44:9 and there are some of whom there is no memory: they are perished, as they that never were; & are born, as not born at all, & their children with them.
text.NABRE: 44:9 Of others no memory remains, for when they perished, they perished, As if they had never lived, they and their children after them.

text.Base: But these also were godly men, whose righteous deeds have not been forgotten;
text.KJV: 44:10 But these were mercifull men, whose righteousnesse hath not beene forgotten.
text.DRV: 44:10 But they are men of mercy, whose godly deeds have not failed:
text.NABRE: 44:10 Yet these also were godly; their virtues have not been forgotten.

text.Base: Their offspring will continue forever, and their glory will never be blotted out.
text.KJV: 44:13 Their seed shall remaine for euer, and their glory shall not be blotted out.
text.DRV: 44:13 and their children because of them abide for ever: their seed and their glory shall not be forsaken.
text.NABRE: 44:13 And for all time their progeny will endure, their glory will never be blotted out;

text.Base: Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on to generation after generation.
text.KJV: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name liueth for euermore.
text.DRV: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, and their name liveth unto generation and generation.
text.NABRE: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on and on.

text.Base: Enoch pleased the Lord and was taken up, an example of repentance to all generations.
text.KJV: 44:16 Enoch pleased the Lord, and was translated, being an example of repentance, to all generations.
text.DRV: 44:16 Henoch pleased God, and was translated into paradise, that he may give repentance to the nations.
text.NABRE: 44:16 The Early Ancestors - [ENOCH walked with the LORD and was taken, that succeeding generations might learn by his example.]

text.Base: Noah was found perfect and righteous; in the time of wrath he kept the race alive.
text.KJV: 44:17 Noah was found perfect and righteous, in the time of wrath, he was taken in exchange (for the world) therefore was he left as a remnant vnto the earth, when the flood came.
text.DRV: 44:17 Noe was found perfect, just, and in the time of wrath he was made a reconciliation.
text.NABRE: 44:17 NOAH, found just and perfect, renewed the race in the time of devastation. Because of his worth there were survivors, and with a sign to him the deluge ended.

text.Base: Abraham was the great father of a multitude of nations, and no one has been found like him in glory.
text.KJV: 44:19 Abraham was a great father of many people: in glory was there none like vnto him:
text.DRV: 44:19 The testaments of the world were made with him, that all flesh should no more be destroyed with the flood.
text.NABRE: 44:19 ABRAHAM, father of many peoples, kept his glory without stain:

text.Base: From Abraham’s descendants the Lord brought forth a godly man, who found favor in the sight of all and was beloved by God and people, Moses, whose memory is blessed.
text.KJV: 45:1 And he brought out of him a mercifull man, which found fauour in the sight of all flesh, euen Moses beloued of God and men, whose memoriall is blessed:
text.DRV: 45:1 Moyses beloved of God, and men: whose memory is in benediction.
text.NABRE: 45:1 Chapter 45 Praise of Moses, Aaron, and Phinehas - From him came the man who would win the favor of all the living: Dear to God and human beings, MOSES, whose memory is a blessing.

text.Base: He made him equal in glory to the holy ones, and made him great, to the terror of his enemies.
text.KJV: 45:2 He made him like to the glorious Saints, and magnified him, so that his enemies stood in feare of him.
text.DRV: 45:2 He made him like in the glory of saints, and magnified him in the fear of his enemies. And with his words he appeased monsters.
text.NABRE: 45:2 God made him like the angels in honor, and strengthened him with fearful powers.

text.Base: He allowed him to hear his voice, and led him into the thick darkness, and gave him the commandments face to face.
text.KJV: 45:5 He made him to heare his voyce, and brought him into the darke cloud, and gaue him commandements before his face, euen the law of life and knowledge, that hee might teach Iacob his Couenants, and Israel his iudgments.
text.DRV: 45:5 For he heard him, and his voice, and brought him into a cloud.
text.NABRE: 45:5 He let him hear his voice, and led him into the cloud, Where he handed over the commandments, the law of life and understanding, That he might teach his precepts to Jacob, his judgments and decrees to Israel.

text.Base: He exalted Aaron, a holy man like Moses who was his brother, of the tribe of Levi.
text.KJV: 45:6 He exalted Aaron an holy man like vnto him, euen his brother, of the tribe of Leui.
text.DRV: 45:6 And he gave him precepts face to face, and a law of life and discipline, to teach Jacob his testament, and Israel his judgements.
text.NABRE: 45:6 He also raised up, like Moses in holiness, his brother AARON, of the tribe of Levi.

text.Base: Moses ordained him and anointed him with holy oil; this was an everlasting covenant for him and for his descendants as long as the heavens endure.
text.KJV: 45:15 Moises consecrated him, and annointed him with holy oile, this was appointed vnto him by an euerlasting couenant, and to his seed so long as the heauens should remaine, that they should minister vnto him, and execute the office of the priesthood, and blesse the people in his name.
text.DRV: 45:15 There were none such so fair before him, even from the beginning.
text.NABRE: 45:15 For Moses ordained him and anointed him with the holy oil, In a lasting covenant with him and his family, as permanent as the heavens, That he should serve God in the priesthood and bless the people in his name.

text.Base: Joshua son of Nun was mighty in war, and was the successor of Moses in prophesying. He became, in accordance with his name, a great savior of God’s elect.
text.KJV: 46:1 Iesus the sonne of Naue was valiant in the wars, and was the successor of Moses in prophesies, who according to his name was made great for the sauing of the elect of God, and taking vengeance of the enemies that rose vp against them, that he might set Israel in their inheritance.
text.DRV: 46:1 Strong in battle was Jesus the son of Nave, successor of Moyses among the prophets, who was great according to his name,
text.NABRE: 46:1 Chapter 46 Joshua, Caleb, the Judges, and Samuel - Valiant warrior was JOSHUA, son of Nun, aide to Moses in the prophetic office, Formed to be, as his name implies, the great savior of God’s chosen ones, To punish the enemy and to give to Israel their heritage.

text.Base: In the days of Moses also he did a loyal deed, he and Caleb son of Jephunneh: they withstood the congregation, restrained the people from sin, and stilled the wicked grumbling.
text.KJV: 46:7 In the time of Moses also, he did a worke of mercie, hee and Caleb the sonne of Iephunne, in that they withstood the Congregation, and withheld the people from sinne, and appeased the wicked murmuring.
text.DRV: 46:7 He made violent assault against the nation of his enemies, and in the going down he destroyed the adversaries,
text.NABRE: 46:7 and showed himself loyal in Moses’ lifetime. He and CALEB, son of Jephunneh, when they opposed the rebel assembly, Averted God’s anger from the people and suppressed the wicked complaint.

text.Base: The judges also, with their respective names, whose hearts did not fall into idolatry and who did not turn away from the Lord—may their memory be blessed!
text.KJV: 46:11 And concerning the Iudges, euery one by name, whose heart went not a whoring, nor departed from the Lord, let their memory be blessed.
text.DRV: 46:11 And our Lord gave strength to Caleb himself, and his strength continued even until old age, so that he went up into the high place of the land, & his seed obtained inheritance.
text.NABRE: 46:11 The JUDGES, each one of them, whose hearts were not deceived, Who did not abandon God— may their memory be ever blessed!

text.Base: Samuel was beloved by his Lord; a prophet of the Lord, he established the kingdom and anointed rulers over his people.
text.KJV: 46:13 Samuel the Prophet of the Lord, beloued of his Lord, established a kingdom, & anointed princes ouer his people.
text.DRV: 46:13 And all the judges by their name, whose heart was not corrupted: which were not turned away from our Lord,
text.NABRE: 46:13 Beloved of his people, dear to his Maker, pledged in a vow from his mother’s womb, As one consecrated to the LORD in the prophetic office, was SAMUEL, the judge who offered sacrifice. At God’s word he established the kingdom and anointed princes to rule the people.

text.Base: Even after he had fallen asleep, he prophesied and foretold to the king his death, and lifted up his voice from the ground in prophecy, to blot out the wickedness of the people.
text.KJV: 46:20 And after his death he prophesied, and shewed the King his end, and lift vp his voyce from the earth in prophesie, to blot out the wickednesse of the people.
text.DRV: 46:20 And our Lord thundered from heaven, and in great sound he made his voice heard,
text.NABRE: 46:20 Even after death his guidance was sought; he made known to the king his fate. From the grave he spoke in prophecy to put an end to wickedness.

text.Base: After him Nathan rose up to prophesy in the days of David.
text.KJV: 47:1 And after him rose vp Nathan to prophesie in the time of Dauid.
text.DRV: 47:1 After these things arose Nathan the Prophet in the days of David.
text.NABRE: 47:1 Chapter 47 Nathan, David, and Solomon - After him came NATHAN who served in David’s presence.

text.Base: As the fat is set apart from the offering of well-being, so David was set apart from the Israelites.
text.KJV: 47:2 As is the fat taken away from the peace offering, so was Dauid chosen out of the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 47:2 And as the fat separated from the flesh, so was David from the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 47:2 Like the choice fat of sacred offerings, so was DAVID in Israel.

text.Base: In all that he did he gave thanks to the Holy One, the Most High, with ascriptions of glory; he sang praise with all his heart, and he loved his Maker.
text.KJV: 47:8 In all his workes hee praised the holy one most High, with words of glory, with his whole heart he sung songs, and loued him that made him.
text.DRV: 47:8 for he destroyed the enemies on every side, and rooted out the Philisthiims the adversaries even until this present day: he brake their horn for ever.
text.NABRE: 47:8 With his every deed he offered thanks to God Most High, in words of praise. With his whole heart he loved his Maker

text.Base: The Lord took away his sins, and exalted his power forever; he gave him a covenant of kingship and a glorious throne in Israel.
text.KJV: 47:11 The Lord tooke away his sinnes, and exalted his horne for euer: he gaue him a couenant of kings, and a throne of glory in Israel.
text.DRV: 47:11 and he made singers to stand before the altar, and by their sound he made sweet tunes.
text.NABRE: 47:11 The LORD forgave him his sins and exalted his strength forever; He conferred on him the rights of royalty and established his throne in Israel.

text.Base: After him a wise son rose up who because of him lived in security.
text.KJV: 47:12 After him rose vp a wise sonne, and for his sake he dwelt at large.
text.DRV: 47:12 And in the solemnities he gave honour, and adorned the times even to the end of his life, that they should praise the holy name of our Lord, and magnify the holiness of God in the morning.
text.NABRE: 47:12 Because of his merits he had as successor a wise son, who lived in security:

text.Base: Solomon reigned in an age of peace, because God gave him rest on every side, so that he might build a house for his name and prepare a sanctuary to stand forever.
text.KJV: 47:13 Salomon reigned in a peaceable time, and was honoured; for God made all quiet round about him, that hee might build an house in his Name, and prepare his Sanctuary for euer.
text.DRV: 47:13 Our Lord purged his sins, and exalted his horn for ever: and he gave him a testament of the kingdom, and the seat of glory in Israel.
text.NABRE: 47:13 SOLOMON reigned during an era of peace, for God brought rest to all his borders. He built a house to the name of God, and established a lasting sanctuary.

text.Base: How wise you were when you were young! You overflowed like the Nile with understanding.
text.KJV: 47:14 How wise wast thou in thy youth, & as a flood filled with vnderstanding.
text.DRV: 47:14 After him arose a wise son, and for him did he overthrow all the might of the enemies.
text.NABRE: 47:14 How wise you were when you were young, overflowing with instruction, like the Nile in flood!

text.Base: Then Elijah arose, a prophet like fire, and his word burned like a torch.
text.KJV: 48:1 Then stood vp Elias the Prophet as fire, and his word burnt like a lampe.
text.DRV: 48:1 And there arose Elias the prophet, as it were fire, and his word burnt as a little torch.
text.NABRE: 48:1 Chapter 48 - Until like fire a prophet appeared, his words a flaming furnace.

text.Base: How glorious you were, Elijah, in your wondrous deeds! Whose glory is equal to yours?
text.KJV: 48:4 O Elias, how wast thou honoured in thy wondrous deedes! and who may glory like vnto thee!
text.DRV: 48:4 So was Elias magnified in his marvelous works. And who can so glory like unto thee?
text.NABRE: 48:4 How awesome are you, ELIJAH! Whose glory is equal to yours?

text.Base: Happy are those who saw you and were adorned with your love! For we also shall surely live.
text.KJV: 48:11 Blessed are they that saw thee, and slept in loue, for we shal surely liue.
text.DRV: 48:11 Blessed are they that saw thee, and were honoured in thy friendship.
text.NABRE: 48:11 Blessed is the one who shall have seen you before he dies!

text.Base: When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, Elisha was filled with his spirit; he performed twice as many signs, and marvels with every utterance of his mouth.
text.KJV: 48:12 Elias it was, who was couered with a whirlewinde: and Elizeus was filled with his spirit: whilest he liued he was not mooued of any prince, neither could any bring him into subiection.
text.DRV: 48:12 For we live by life only, but after death our name shall not be such.
text.NABRE: 48:12 When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, ELISHA was filled with his spirit; He worked twice as many marvels, and every utterance of his mouth was wonderful. During his lifetime he feared no one, nor was anyone able to intimidate his will.

text.Base: Nothing was too hard for him, and when he was dead, his body prophesied.
text.KJV: 48:13 No word could ouercome him, & after his death his body prophecied.
text.DRV: 48:13 Elias was indeed hid in the whirlwind, & his spirit was complete in Eliseus: in his days he feared not the Prince, and no man overcame him by might.
text.NABRE: 48:13 Nothing was beyond his power; and from where he lay buried, his body prophesied.

text.Base: But they called upon the Lord who is merciful, spreading out their hands toward him. The Holy One quickly heard them from heaven, and delivered them by the hand of Isaiah.
text.KJV: 48:20 But they called vpon the Lord which is mercifull, and stretched out their hands towards him, and immediatly the holy One heard them out of heauen, and deliuered them by the ministery of Esay.
text.DRV: 48:20 In his days came up Sennacherib, and sent Rabsaces, and lifted up his hand against them, and put forth his hand upon Sion, and became proud by his mightiness.
text.NABRE: 48:20 But they called upon the Most High God and lifted up their hands to him; He heard the prayer they uttered, and saved them through ISAIAH.

text.Base: For Hezekiah did what was pleasing to the Lord, and he kept firmly to the ways of his ancestor David, as he was commanded by the prophet Isaiah, who was great and trustworthy in his visions.
text.KJV: 48:22 For Ezekias had done the thing that pleased the Lord, and was strong in the wayes of Dauid his father, as Esay the Prophet, who was great and faithfull in his vision, had commaunded him.
text.DRV: 48:22 And they invocated our merciful Lord, and spreading their hands, they lifted them up to heaven, and the holy Lord God quickly heard their voice.
text.NABRE: 48:22 For Hezekiah did what was right and held fast to the paths of David, As ordered by the illustrious prophet Isaiah, who saw truth in visions.

text.Base: The memory of Josiah is like a blending of incense prepared by the art of the perfumer; it is sweet as honey to every mouth, and like music at a banquet of wine.
text.KJV: 49:1 The remembrance of Iosias is like the composition of the perfume þ is made by the arte of the Apothecarie: it is sweete as hony in all mouthes, and as musicke at a banquet of wine.
text.DRV: 49:1 The memory of Josias is according to the confection of perfume made by the work of an apothecary.
text.NABRE: 49:1 Chapter 49 Josiah and the Prophets - The name JOSIAH is like blended incense, made lasting by a skilled perfumer. Precious is his memory, like honey to the taste, like music at a banquet.

text.Base: Except for David and Hezekiah and Josiah, all of them were great sinners, for they abandoned the law of the Most High; the kings of Judah came to an end.
text.KJV: 49:4 All, except Dauid and Ezechias, and Iosias, were defectiue: for they forsooke the Law of the most High, (euen) the kings of Iudah failed:
text.DRV: 49:4 And he governed his heart toward our Lord, and in the days of sinners he strengthened piety.
text.NABRE: 49:4 Except for David, Hezekiah, and Josiah, they all were wicked; They abandoned the Law of the Most High, these kings of Judah, right to the very end.

text.Base: It was Ezekiel who saw the vision of glory, which God showed him above the chariot of the cherubim.
text.KJV: 49:8 It was Ezechiel who sawe the glorious vision, which was shewed him vpon the chariot of the Cherubims
text.DRV: 49:8 They burnt the chosen city of holiness, & made the ways thereof desolate in the hand of Jeremie.
text.NABRE: 49:8 EZEKIEL beheld a vision and described the different creatures of the chariot;

text.Base: May the bones of the twelve prophets send forth new life from where they lie, for they comforted the people of Jacob and delivered them with confident hope.
text.KJV: 49:10 And of the twelue prophets let the memorial be blessed, and let their bones flourish againe out of their place: for they comforted Iacob, and deliuered them by assured hope.
text.DRV: 49:10 Ezechiel who saw the sight of glory, which he shewed him in the chariot of Cherubs.
text.NABRE: 49:10 Then, too, the TWELVE PROPHETS— may their bones flourish with new life where they lie!— They gave new strength to Jacob and saved him with steadfast hope.

text.Base: How shall we magnify Zerubbabel? He was like a signet ring on the right hand;
text.KJV: 49:11 How shall we magnifie Zorobabel? euen he was as a signet on the right hand.
text.DRV: 49:11 For he made mention of the enemies in rain, to do good unto them, that have shewed right ways.
text.NABRE: 49:11 The Heroes After the Exile - How to extol ZERUBBABEL? He was like a signet ring on the right hand,

text.Base: so too was Joshua son of Jozadak; in their days they built the house and raised a temple holy to the Lord, prepared for everlasting glory.
text.KJV: 49:12 So was Iesus the sonne of Iosedec: who in their time builded the house, and set vp an holy Temple to the Lord, which was prepared for euerlasting glory.
text.DRV: 49:12 And the bones of the twelve prophets well may they spring out of their place: for they have strengthened Jacob, and have redeemed themselves in the fidelity of power.
text.NABRE: 49:12 And JESHUA, Jozadak’s son? In their time they rebuilt the altar And erected the holy temple, destined for everlasting glory.

text.Base: The memory of Nehemiah also is lasting; he raised our fallen walls, and set up gates and bars, and rebuilt our ruined houses.
text.KJV: 49:13 And among the elect was Neemias whose renowme is great, who raised vp for vs, the walles that were fallen, and set vp the gates & the barres, and raised vp our ruines againe.
text.DRV: 49:13 How may we magnify Zorobabel? For he also was as a signet on the right hand,
text.NABRE: 49:13 Exalted be the memory of NEHEMIAH! He rebuilt our ruined walls, Restored our shattered defenses, and set up gates and bars.

text.Base: The leader of his brothers and the pride of his people was the high priest, Simon son of Onias, who in his life repaired the house, and in his time fortified the temple.
text.KJV: 50:1 Simon the high priest the sonne of Onias, who in his life repaired the house againe, and in his dayes fortified the Temple:
text.DRV: 50:1 Simon the son of Onias, the high priest, who in his life held up the house, and in his days strengthened the temple.
text.NABRE: 50:1 Chapter 50 Simeon, Son of Jochanan - Greatest of his family, the glory of his people, was SIMEON the priest, son of Jochanan, In whose time the house of God was renovated, in whose days the temple was reinforced.

text.Base: How glorious he was, surrounded by the people, as he came out of the house of the curtain.
text.KJV: 50:5 How was he honoured in the midst of the people, in his comming out of the Sanctuarie?
text.DRV: 50:5 Who prevailed to amplify the city, who obtained glory in conversing with the nation: and amplified the entrance of the house, and the court.
text.NABRE: 50:5 How splendid he was as he looked out from the tent, as he came from behind the veil!

text.Base: And now bless the God of all, who everywhere works great wonders, who fosters our growth from birth, and deals with us according to his mercy.
text.KJV: 50:22 Now therefore blesse yee the God of all, which onely doth wonderous things euery where, which exalteth our daies from the wombe, and dealeth with vs according to his mercy.
text.DRV: 50:22 Then coming down, he lifted up his hands over all the congregation of the children of Israel, to give glory to God from his lips, and to glory in his name,
text.NABRE: 50:22 And now, bless the God of all, who has done wonders on earth; Who fosters growth from the womb, fashioning it according to his will!

text.Base: May he give us gladness of heart, and may there be peace in our days in Israel, as in the days of old.
text.KJV: 50:23 He grant vs ioyfulnesse of heart, and that peace may be in our daies in Israel for euer.
text.DRV: 50:23 and he repeated his prayer, willing to shew the power of God.
text.NABRE: 50:23 May he grant you a wise heart and abide with you in peace;

text.Base: Instruction in understanding and knowledge I have written in this book, I, Jesus son of Eleazar son of Sirach of Jerusalem, whose heart poured forth wisdom.
text.KJV: 50:27 Iesus the sonne of Sirach of Hierusalem hath written in this booke, the instruction of vnderstanding and knowledge, who out of his heart powred forth wisedome.
text.DRV: 50:27 Two nations my soul hateth: and the third is no nation, which I hate:
text.NABRE: 50:27 Wise instruction, appropriate proverbs, I have written in this book— I, Yeshua Ben Eleazar Ben Sira— as they poured forth from my heart’s understanding.

text.Base: I will give thanks to you, O Lord and King, and praise you, O God my Savior. I give thanks to your name;
text.KJV: 51:1 I will thanke thee, O Lord and king, and praise thee O God my Sauiour, I doe giue praise vnto thy name:
text.DRV: 51:1 The prayer of Jesus the son of Sirach. I will confess to thee o Lord King, and will praise thee God my Savior.
text.NABRE: 51:1 Chapter 51 A Prayer of Thanksgiving - I give you thanks, LORD and King, I praise you, God my savior! I declare your name, refuge of my life,

text.Base: for you have been my protector and helper and have delivered me from destruction.
text.KJV: 51:2 For thou art my defeuder, and helper, and hast preserued my body from destruction, and from the snare of the slanderous tongue, and from the lippes that forge lies, and hast beene my helper against mine aduersaries.
text.DRV: 51:2 I will confess to thy name: because thou art become my helper and protector,
text.NABRE: 51:2 because you have ransomed my life from death; You held back my body from the pit, and delivered my foot from the power of Sheol. You have preserved me from the scourge of the slanderous tongue, and from the lips of those who went over to falsehood. You were with me against those who rise up against me;

text.Base: While I was still young, before I went on my travels, I sought wisdom openly in my prayer.
text.KJV: 51:13 When I was yet yong, or euer I went abroad, I desired wisedome openly in my prayer.
text.DRV: 51:13 Thou hast exalted my habitation upon the earth, and I have prayed for death to pass away.
text.NABRE: 51:13 Ben Sira’s Pursuit of Wisdom - When I was young and innocent, I sought wisdom.

text.Base: I made progress in her; to him who gives me wisdom I will give glory.
text.KJV: 51:17 I profited therein, will I ascribe the glory vnto him that giueth me wisedome:
text.DRV: 51:17 Therefore will I confess, & say praise to thee, and bless the name of our Lord.
text.NABRE: 51:17 Since in this way I have profited, I will give my Teacher grateful praise.

text.Base: Draw near to me, you who are uneducated, and lodge in the house of instruction.
text.KJV: 51:23 Draw neere vnto me you vnlearned, and dwell in the house of learning.
text.DRV: 51:23 To him that giveth me wisdom, will I give glory.
text.NABRE: 51:23 Come aside to me, you untutored, and take up lodging in the house of instruction;

text.Base: Put your neck under her yoke, and let your souls receive instruction; it is to be found close by.
text.KJV: 51:26 Put your necke vnder the yoke, and let your soule receiue instruction, she is hard at hand to finde.
text.DRV: 51:26 Istretched forth my hands on high, & I lamented foolishness.
text.NABRE: 51:26 Take her yoke upon your neck; that your mind may receive her teaching. For she is close to those who seek her, and the one who is in earnest finds her.

text.Base: See with your own eyes that I have labored but little and found for myself much serenity.
text.KJV: 51:27 Behold with your eies, how that I haue had but little labour, and haue gotten vnto me much rest.
text.DRV: 51:27 I directed my soul to wisdom, and in knowledge I found it.
text.NABRE: 51:27 See for yourselves! I have labored only a little, but have found much.

text.Base: Do your work in good time, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
text.KJV: 51:30 Worke your worke betimes, & in his time he will giue you your reward.
text.DRV: 51:30 Our Lord hath given me a tongue for my reward: and with the same I will praise him.
text.NABRE: 51:30 Work at your tasks in due season, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
